/**
 * @bim-ai/icons — BIM-native SVG icon library.
 *
 * Drop-in compatible with lucide-react's icon API:
 *
 *   import { WallIcon, DoorIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons'
 *   <WallIcon size={18} strokeWidth={1.5} className="text-stone-600" />
 *
 * All icons are 24×24 viewBox, stroke-based, currentColor.
 * Props: size, strokeWidth, absoluteStrokeWidth, plus any SVG attribute.
 */

export type { BimIcon, BimIconProps, BimIconHifiProps } from './icon';

// High-fidelity 48×48 icons
export {
  WallHifi,
  DoorHifi,
  WindowHifi,
  StairHifi,
  ColumnHifi,
  BeamHifi,
  RoofHifi,
  RoomHifi,
  SectionHifi,
  FloorHifi,
} from './hifi';

// Phase 1 — drawing tools
export {
  WallIcon,
  DoorIcon,
  WindowIcon,
  FloorIcon,
  RoofIcon,
  StairIcon,
  RailingIcon,
  RoomIcon,
  DimensionIcon,
  SectionIcon,
  TagIcon,
  CurtainWallIcon,
  ColumnIcon,
  BeamIcon,
} from './tools';

// Phase 2 — views & annotations (original)
export {
  PlanViewIcon,
  SectionViewIcon,
  ElevationViewIcon,
  OrbitViewIcon,
  SheetIcon,
  ScheduleViewIcon,
  CalloutIcon,
  ViewpointIcon,
  SectionBoxIcon,
  GridLineIcon,
  LevelIcon,
  DetailLineIcon,
} from './views';

// Phase 3 — organization & coordination (original)
export {
  FamilyIcon,
  FamilyTypeIcon,
  GroupIcon,
  AssemblyIcon,
  LinkedModelIcon,
  MaterialIcon,
  WallLayerIcon,
  PhaseIcon,
  IssueIcon,
  ClashIcon,
  ValidationRuleIcon,
  DeviationIcon,
} from './organization';

// MEP — mechanical, electrical & plumbing
export {
  DuctRectIcon,
  DuctRoundIcon,
  PipeIcon,
  CableTrayIcon,
  ConduitIcon,
  MechanicalEquipmentIcon,
  PlumbingFixtureIcon,
  LightingFixtureIcon,
  ElectricalPanelIcon,
  FireSprinklerIcon,
  DiffuserIcon,
  MepSpaceIcon,
} from './mep';

// Structural
export {
  FoundationIcon,
  StripFootingIcon,
  TrussIcon,
  BraceIcon,
  RebarIcon,
  StructuralConnectionIcon,
} from './structural';

// Architectural additions
export {
  CeilingIcon,
  OpeningIcon,
  ShaftOpeningIcon,
  RampIcon,
  MassIcon,
  CurtainPanelIcon,
  MullionIcon,
  SkyLightIcon,
  PartitionIcon,
} from './architectural';

// Site & civil
export {
  TopoIcon,
  PropertyLineIcon,
  ParkingSpaceIcon,
  PlantingIcon,
  RoadIcon,
  RetainingWallIcon,
  NorthArrowIcon,
} from './site';

// Annotation & documentation
export {
  RevisionCloudIcon,
  BreakLineIcon,
  CentreLineIcon,
  KeynoteIcon,
  MatchLineIcon,
  ScaleBarIcon,
  AreaLabelIcon,
  SpotElevationIcon,
  SlopeArrowIcon,
} from './annotation';

// Workflow, coordination & data
export {
  WorksetIcon,
  DesignOptionIcon,
  RevisionIcon,
  RFIIcon,
  SubmittalIcon,
  PointCloudIcon,
  DigitalTwinIcon,
  SyncIcon,
  TransmittalIcon,
  IFCIcon,
  LODIcon,
  QuantityTakeoffIcon,
  EnergyModelIcon,
  ScopeBoxIcon,
} from './workflow';

// Edit / geometry operations
export {
  MirrorIcon,
  ArrayLinearIcon,
  ArrayRadialIcon,
  AlignIcon,
  SplitIcon,
  TrimExtendIcon,
  VoidIcon,
  PinIcon,
  UnpinIcon,
} from './edit';

// Architectural extended
export {
  WallSweepIcon,
  WallRevealIcon,
  ComponentIcon,
  RoofExtrusionIcon,
  RoofSoffitIcon,
  FasciaIcon,
  GutterIcon,
  CurtainSystemIcon,
  CurtainGridIcon,
  VerticalOpeningIcon,
  DormerOpeningIcon,
  FaceOpeningIcon,
  RoomSeparatorIcon,
  AreaBoundaryIcon,
  ReferencePlaneIcon,
  WorkPlaneIcon,
  ModelLineIcon,
  ModelTextIcon,
  StairPathIcon,
} from './architectural-extended';

// Structural extended
export {
  BeamSystemIcon,
  FoundationSlabIcon,
  SlabEdgeIcon,
  AreaReinforcementIcon,
  PathReinforcementIcon,
  FabricSheetIcon,
  ShearStudIcon,
  AnchorIcon,
  BoltIcon,
  WeldIcon,
  AnalyticalNodeIcon,
  StructuralLoadIcon,
  BoundaryConditionIcon,
} from './structural-extended';

// MEP extended
export {
  FlexDuctIcon,
  DuctFittingIcon,
  DuctAccessoryIcon,
  DuctInsulationIcon,
  DuctLiningIcon,
  FlexPipeIcon,
  PipeFittingIcon,
  PipeAccessoryIcon,
  PipeInsulationIcon,
  CableTrayFittingIcon,
  ConduitFittingIcon,
  ElectricalWireIcon,
  SwitchSystemIcon,
  LightingSwitchIcon,
  CommunicationDeviceIcon,
  FireAlarmDeviceIcon,
  DataDeviceIcon,
  NurseCallIcon,
  SecurityDeviceIcon,
  HVACZoneIcon,
  PanelScheduleIcon,
  DuctSystemIcon,
  PipingSystemIcon,
  ValveIcon,
} from './mep-extended';

// Views extended
export {
  ReflectedCeilingPlanIcon,
  StructuralPlanIcon,
  AreaPlanIcon,
  DetailViewIcon,
  DraftingViewIcon,
  WalkthroughIcon,
  LegendIcon,
  SheetListIcon,
  NoteBlockIcon,
  ViewListIcon,
  PanelScheduleViewIcon,
  GraphicalColumnScheduleIcon,
  ViewReferenceIcon,
} from './views-extended';

// Annotation extended
export {
  AngularDimensionIcon,
  RadialDimensionIcon,
  ArcLengthDimensionIcon,
  OrdinalDimensionIcon,
  SpotCoordinateIcon,
  ElevationTagIcon,
  FilledRegionIcon,
  MaskingRegionIcon,
  DetailComponentIcon,
  RepeatingDetailIcon,
  InsulationAnnotationIcon,
  TextAnnotationIcon,
  MultiCategoryTagIcon,
  MaterialTagIcon,
  RoomTagIcon,
  SpaceTagIcon,
  AreaTagIcon,
  StairTagIcon,
  SymbolIcon,
  StairPathAnnotationIcon,
  ReferencePointIcon,
} from './annotation-extended';

// Edit basic / modify operations
export {
  MoveIcon,
  CopyIcon,
  RotateIcon,
  ScaleIcon,
  OffsetIcon,
  DeleteIcon,
  JoinGeometryIcon,
  UnjoinGeometryIcon,
  SwitchJoinOrderIcon,
  WallJoinsIcon,
  PaintMaterialIcon,
  MeasureBetweenIcon,
  MeasureAlongIcon,
  UngroupIcon,
} from './edit-basic';

// Collaboration & project management
export {
  ReloadLatestIcon,
  EditingRequestIcon,
  CopyMonitorIcon,
  CoordinationReviewIcon,
  PurgeUnusedIcon,
  TransferProjectStandardsIcon,
  ProjectInfoIcon,
  ProjectParametersIcon,
  SharedParametersIcon,
  ObjectStylesIcon,
} from './collaboration';

content.text:
The root cause isn't pixel count — it's that 7 of 10 icons have only **one fill zone** by design:

- WallHifi = one concrete rect
- StairHifi = concrete treads, all same material
- ColumnHifi = one concrete shaft
- FloorHifi = one concrete slab
- BeamHifi = one steel rect
- RoofHifi = one roof plane
- RoomHifi = one room fill

CSS variables cascade correctly; there's just nothing for a second color to attach to.

**What would actually improve the Color mode, in order of impact:**

1. **Multi-material redesigns** (biggest delta, no canvas change needed)
   - Wall: outer plaster face + concrete core + inner plaster face — three visible layers
   - Column: steel cap/base plates + concrete shaft
   - Floor: tile/screed topping layer above structural slab
   - Stair: concrete risers + distinct tread nosing material
   - Beam: steel web + flange plates differentiated

2. **96×96 canvas** — more room to show layer thickness properly; 48px makes a 3-layer wall ambiguous

3. **SVG `<linearGradient>`** inside `<defs>` — steel sheen, glass sky reflection, adds perceived depth

4. **Per-material stroke colors** in Color mode — concrete outlines should be `#7a7570` not black `currentColor`; glass frame should be `#38bdf8` tinted

The single highest-leverage change is redesigning the icons to show multiple materials. Want me to do that now — keep the 48×48 canvas, but redesign all 10 icons so Color mode is visually distinct?

lastPrompt: no color still isnt good because it is one color only; what would be needed to further improve the design / quality? even more px?

 * High-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons with multi-material fills.
 *
 * Each icon uses two or more material zones so the Color variant
 * shows distinct, architecture-accurate palettes.
 *
 * CSS custom properties (set on a wrapper element):
 *   --hi-concrete / --hi-concrete-op  — structural concrete
 *   --hi-finish   / --hi-finish-op    — plaster, screed, paint face
 *   --hi-glass    / --hi-glass-op     — glazing panes
 *   --hi-steel    / --hi-steel-op     — steel sections, rebar
 *   --hi-leaf                          — timber: door leaf, window frame, fascia
 *   --hi-roof     / --hi-roof-op      — roof surface
 *   --hi-room     / --hi-room-op      — room interior fill
 *
 * Without variables the icons render in monochrome currentColor.
 *
 * Stroke hierarchy:
 *   strokeWidth 2    — primary outlines
 *   strokeWidth 1.5  — secondary edges, reveals
 *   strokeWidth 0.75 — hatch, detail lines
 */
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';

// ── Wall: 3-layer plan section — finish | concrete core | finish ──────────────
export const WallHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'WallHifi',
  <>
    {/* Outer plaster face */}
    <rect
      x="4" y="18" width="4" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete core */}
    <rect
      x="8" y="18" width="32" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    {/* Inner plaster face */}
    <rect
      x="40" y="18" width="4" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete hatch */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.35}>
      <line x1="8"  y1="24" x2="14" y2="18" />
      <line x1="8"  y1="30" x2="20" y2="18" />
      <line x1="14" y1="30" x2="26" y2="18" />
      <line x1="20" y1="30" x2="32" y2="18" />
      <line x1="26" y1="30" x2="38" y2="18" />
      <line x1="32" y1="30" x2="40" y2="22" />
      <line x1="38" y1="30" x2="40" y2="28" />
    </g>
    {/* Layer dividers */}
    <line x1="8"  y1="18" x2="8"  y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
    <line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
    {/* Main outlines */}
    <line x1="4"  y1="18" x2="44" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="30" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="18" x2="4"  y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="44" y1="18" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
  </>,
);

/**
 * High-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons with multi-material fills.
 *
 * Each icon uses two or more material zones so the Color variant
 * shows distinct, architecture-accurate palettes.
 *
 * CSS custom properties (set on a wrapper element):
 *   --hi-concrete / --hi-concrete-op  — structural concrete
 *   --hi-finish   / --hi-finish-op    — plaster, screed, paint face
 *   --hi-glass    / --hi-glass-op     — glazing panes
 *   --hi-steel    / --hi-steel-op     — steel sections, rebar
 *   --hi-leaf                          — timber: door leaf, window frame, fascia
 *   --hi-roof     / --hi-roof-op      — roof surface
 *   --hi-room     / --hi-room-op      — room interior fill
 *
 * Without variables the icons render in monochrome currentColor.
 *
 * Stroke hierarchy:
 *   strokeWidth 2    — primary outlines
 *   strokeWidth 1.5  — secondary edges, reveals
 *   strokeWidth 0.75 — hatch, detail lines
 */
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';

// ── Wall: 3-layer plan section — finish | concrete core | finish ──────────────
export const WallHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'WallHifi',
  <>
    {/* Outer plaster face */}
    <rect
      x="4" y="18" width="4" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete core */}
    <rect
      x="8" y="18" width="32" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    {/* Inner plaster face */}
    <rect
      x="40" y="18" width="4" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete hatch */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.35}>
      <line x1="8"  y1="24" x2="14" y2="18" />
      <line x1="8"  y1="30" x2="20" y2="18" />
      <line x1="14" y1="30" x2="26" y2="18" />
      <line x1="20" y1="30" x2="32" y2="18" />
      <line x1="26" y1="30" x2="38" y2="18" />
      <line x1="32" y1="30" x2="40" y2="22" />
      <line x1="38" y1="30" x2="40" y2="28" />
    </g>
    {/* Layer dividers */}
    <line x1="8"  y1="18" x2="8"  y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
    <line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
    {/* Main outlines */}
    <line x1="4"  y1="18" x2="44" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="30" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="18" x2="4"  y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="44" y1="18" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
  </>,
);

// ── Door: concrete walls + wood jamb liners + door leaf + swing arc ───────────
export const DoorHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'DoorHifi',
  <>
    {/* Left wall */}
    <rect
      x="4" y="12" width="12" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="4"  y1="20" x2="8"  y2="12" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="24" x2="12" y2="12" />
      <line x1="8"  y1="24" x2="16" y2="16" />
      <line x1="12" y1="24" x2="16" y2="20" />
    </g>
    <line x1="4"  y1="12" x2="16" y2="12" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="24" x2="16" y2="24" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="12" x2="4"  y2="24" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Right wall */}
    <rect
      x="32" y="12" width="12" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="32" y1="20" x2="36" y2="12" />
      <line x1="32" y1="24" x2="40" y2="12" />
      <line x1="36" y1="24" x2="44" y2="16" />
      <line x1="40" y1="24" x2="44" y2="20" />
    </g>
    <line x1="32" y1="12" x2="44" y2="12" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="32" y1="24" x2="44" y2="24" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="44" y1="12" x2="44" y2="24" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Wood jamb liners at reveals */}
    <rect x="15" y="12" width="2" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5} stroke="none" />
    <rect x="31" y="12" width="2" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5} stroke="none" />

    {/* Door jamb lines */}
    <line x1="16" y1="12" x2="16" y2="24" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="32" y1="12" x2="32" y2="24" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Door leaf */}
    <rect x="14.5" y="24" width="3" height="16"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} />

    {/* Swing arc */}
    <path d="M32 24 A16 16 0 0 1 16 40" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />

    {/* Hinge pins */}
    <circle cx="16" cy="25.5" r="1.2" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />
    <circle cx="16" cy="38.5" r="1.2" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />
  </>,
);

// ── Window: concrete walls + wood frame sections + double-pane glass ──────────
export const WindowHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'WindowHifi',
  <>
    {/* Left wall */}
    <rect
      x="4" y="14" width="10" height="20"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="4"  y1="22" x2="8"  y2="14" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="28" x2="12" y2="14" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="34" x2="14" y2="22" />
      <line x1="8"  y1="34" x2="14" y2="28" />
      <line x1="12" y1="34" x2="14" y2="32" />
    </g>
    <line x1="4"  y1="14" x2="14" y2="14" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="34" x2="14" y2="34" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="14" x2="4"  y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Right wall */}
    <rect
      x="34" y="14" width="10" height="20"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="34" y1="22" x2="38" y2="14" />
      <line x1="34" y1="28" x2="42" y2="14" />
      <line x1="34" y1="34" x2="44" y2="22" />
      <line x1="38" y1="34" x2="44" y2="28" />
      <line x1="42" y1="34" x2="44" y2="32" />
    </g>
    <line x1="34" y1="14" x2="44" y2="14" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="34" y1="34" x2="44" y2="34" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="44" y1="14" x2="44" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Wood frame — outer section (y=14..21) and inner section (y=27..34) */}
    <rect x="14" y="14" width="20" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.35} stroke="none" />
    <rect x="14" y="27" width="20" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.35} stroke="none" />

    {/* Frame outlines */}
    <line x1="14" y1="14" x2="34" y2="14" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="14" y1="34" x2="34" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="14" y1="14" x2="14" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="34" y1="14" x2="34" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Glass: double pane */}
    <rect x="14" y="21" width="20" height="6"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-glass, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-glass-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    <line x1="14" y1="21" x2="34" y2="21" strokeWidth={1.2} />
    <line x1="14" y1="27" x2="34" y2="27" strokeWidth={1.2} />
  </>,
);

// ── Stair: plan view — concrete body + tread finish strips + arrow ─────────────
export const StairHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'StairHifi',
  <>
    {/* Concrete structural body */}
    <path
      d="M42 10H34V18H26V26H18V34H10V42H42Z"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />

    {/* Tread finish strips */}
    <rect x="34" y="10" width="8" height="2.5"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.5)' }} />
    <rect x="26" y="18" width="8" height="2.5"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.5)' }} />
    <rect x="18" y="26" width="8" height="2.5"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.5)' }} />
    <rect x="10" y="34" width="8" height="2.5"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.5)' }} />

    {/* Nosing lines */}
    <line x1="34" y1="10" x2="34" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="26" y1="18" x2="26" y2="26" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="18" y1="26" x2="18" y2="34" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="10" y1="34" x2="10" y2="42" strokeWidth={2} />

    {/* Outer boundary */}
    <path d="M42 10H34V18H26V26H18V34H10V42H42Z" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />

    {/* Cut line */}
    <line x1="10" y1="10" x2="42" y2="42" strokeWidth={1.5} strokeDasharray="4 3" />

    {/* Direction arrow */}
    <line x1="22" y1="32" x2="32" y2="20" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <polygon points="32,20 25,22 27,28" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />
  </>,
);

// ── Column: plan section — concrete with steel rebar at corners ───────────────
export const ColumnHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'ColumnHifi',
  <>
    {/* Column body */}
    <rect
      x="14" y="14" width="20" height="20"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete hatch */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="14" y1="22" x2="20" y2="14" />
      <line x1="14" y1="28" x2="26" y2="14" />
      <line x1="14" y1="34" x2="32" y2="14" />
      <line x1="18" y1="34" x2="34" y2="18" />
      <line x1="24" y1="34" x2="34" y2="24" />
      <line x1="30" y1="34" x2="34" y2="30" />
    </g>
    {/* Bold border */}
    <rect x="14" y="14" width="20" height="20" strokeWidth={2} />
    {/* Rebar circles (steel) */}
    <g style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.85} stroke="none">
      <circle cx="17.5" cy="17.5" r="1.5" />
      <circle cx="30.5" cy="17.5" r="1.5" />
      <circle cx="17.5" cy="30.5" r="1.5" />
      <circle cx="30.5" cy="30.5" r="1.5" />
    </g>
    {/* Grid lines */}
    <line x1="24" y1="4"  x2="24" y2="14" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
    <line x1="24" y1="34" x2="24" y2="41" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
    <line x1="4"  y1="24" x2="14" y2="24" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
    <line x1="34" y1="24" x2="44" y2="24" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
    {/* Grid bubble */}
    <circle cx="24" cy="4" r="4" strokeWidth={1} />
  </>,
);

// ── Beam: composite — concrete slab on steel I-section ────────────────────────
export const BeamHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'BeamHifi',
  <>
    {/* Concrete slab on top */}
    <rect
      x="4" y="4" width="40" height="10"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.35}>
      <line x1="4"  y1="6"  x2="6"  y2="4" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="14" x2="14" y2="4" />
      <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="20" y2="4" />
      <line x1="16" y1="14" x2="26" y2="4" />
      <line x1="22" y1="14" x2="32" y2="4" />
      <line x1="28" y1="14" x2="38" y2="4" />
      <line x1="34" y1="14" x2="44" y2="4" />
      <line x1="40" y1="14" x2="44" y2="10" />
    </g>
    <rect x="4" y="4" width="40" height="10" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />

    {/* Steel top flange */}
    <rect
      x="8" y="14" width="32" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }}
    />
    <rect x="8" y="14" width="32" height="7" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />

    {/* Web */}
    <rect
      x="19.5" y="21" width="9" height="12"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }}
    />
    <line x1="19.5" y1="21" x2="19.5" y2="33" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="28.5" y1="21" x2="28.5" y2="33" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Steel bottom flange */}
    <rect
      x="8" y="33" width="32" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }}
    />
    <rect x="8" y="33" width="32" height="7" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />

    {/* Fillet radii */}
    <path d="M19.5 21 Q19.5 24 22 24" strokeWidth={1} />
    <path d="M28.5 21 Q28.5 24 26 24" strokeWidth={1} />
    <path d="M19.5 33 Q19.5 30 22 30" strokeWidth={1} />
    <path d="M28.5 33 Q28.5 30 26 30" strokeWidth={1} />
  </>,
);

// ── Roof: tile surface + ridge beam + wood fascia ─────────────────────────────
export const RoofHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'RoofHifi',
  <>
    {/* Main roof surface */}
    <path
      d="M24 7L44 43L4 43Z"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-roof, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-roof-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    {/* Tile course lines */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.5}>
      <line x1="21" y1="14" x2="27" y2="14" />
      <line x1="17" y1="21" x2="31" y2="21" />
      <line x1="13" y1="28" x2="35" y2="28" />
      <line x1="9"  y1="35" x2="39" y2="35" />
    </g>
    {/* Slopes */}
    <line x1="24" y1="7" x2="4"  y2="43" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="24" y1="7" x2="44" y2="43" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    {/* Eave */}
    <line x1="4" y1="43" x2="44" y2="43" strokeWidth={2} />
    {/* Wood fascia board at eave */}
    <rect x="3" y="43" width="42" height="4"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5} strokeWidth={1} />
    {/* Wood ridge cap */}
    <rect x="21" y="5" width="6" height="4"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5} strokeWidth={1} />
    <line x1="21" y1="7" x2="27" y2="7" strokeWidth={2} />
  </>,
);

// ── Room: plan view — concrete wall bands + room interior ─────────────────────
export const RoomHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'RoomHifi',
  <>
    {/* Wall band fills — top, bottom, left, right */}
    <rect x="5" y="5"  width="38" height="6"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
    <rect x="5" y="37" width="38" height="6"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
    <rect x="5" y="11" width="6"  height="26"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
    <rect x="37" y="11" width="6" height="26"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />

    {/* Room interior fill */}
    <rect x="11" y="11" width="26" height="26"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-room, transparent)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-room-op, 1)' }} />

    {/* Outer wall boundary */}
    <rect x="5" y="5" width="38" height="38" strokeWidth={2} />
    {/* Inner room boundary */}
    <rect x="11" y="11" width="26" height="26"
      strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="4 3" strokeOpacity={0.6} />

    {/* Centroid cross */}
    <line x1="24" y1="17" x2="24" y2="31" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="17" y1="24" x2="31" y2="24" strokeWidth={1.5} />

    {/* Label placeholder lines */}
    <line x1="16" y1="33" x2="32" y2="33" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.45} />
  </>,
);

// ── Section: cut marker through a concrete wall ────────────────────────────────
export const SectionHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'SectionHifi',
  <>
    {/* Concrete wall being cut — above and below the cut line */}
    <rect x="14" y="11" width="20" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
    <rect x="14" y="18" width="20" height="7"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
    {/* Sparse hatch in upper wall zone */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.3}>
      <line x1="14" y1="15" x2="19" y2="11" />
      <line x1="14" y1="18" x2="23" y2="11" />
      <line x1="20" y1="18" x2="29" y2="11" />
      <line x1="26" y1="18" x2="34" y2="12" />
    </g>
    <line x1="14" y1="11" x2="34" y2="11" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.4} />
    <line x1="14" y1="25" x2="34" y2="25" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.4} />

    {/* Cut line */}
    <line x1="14" y1="18" x2="34" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} strokeDasharray="5 3" />

    {/* Left end bubble */}
    <circle cx="10" cy="18" r="5" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="10" y1="23" x2="10" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <polygon points="7,34 13,34 10,41" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />

    {/* Right end bubble */}
    <circle cx="38" cy="18" r="5" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <line x1="38" y1="23" x2="38" y2="34" strokeWidth={1.5} />
    <polygon points="35,34 41,34 38,41" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />

    {/* Divider lines inside bubbles */}
    <line x1="6"  y1="18" x2="14" y2="18" strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
    <line x1="34" y1="18" x2="42" y2="18" strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
  </>,
);

// ── Floor: screed topping + structural concrete slab ──────────────────────────
export const FloorHifi = bimIconHifi(
  'FloorHifi',
  <>
    {/* Screed / finish topping */}
    <rect x="4" y="10" width="40" height="6"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }}
    />
    <line x1="4"  y1="10" x2="44" y2="10" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="16" x2="44" y2="16" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />

    {/* Structural slab */}
    <rect x="4" y="16" width="40" height="22"
      style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}
    />
    {/* Concrete hatch */}
    <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.4}>
      <line x1="4"  y1="24" x2="12" y2="16" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="30" x2="18" y2="16" />
      <line x1="4"  y1="36" x2="24" y2="16" />
      <line x1="6"  y1="38" x2="30" y2="16" />
      <line x1="12" y1="38" x2="36" y2="16" />
      <line x1="18" y1="38" x2="42" y2="18" />
      <line x1="24" y1="38" x2="44" y2="22" />
      <line x1="30" y1="38" x2="44" y2="28" />
      <line x1="36" y1="38" x2="44" y2="34" />
    </g>
    {/* Slab boundary */}
    <line x1="4"  y1="38" x2="44" y2="38" strokeWidth={2} />
    <line x1="4"  y1="10" x2="4"  y2="38" strokeWidth={1.5} />

      strokeWidth={0.75} strokeDasharray="3 2" strokeOpacity={0.5} />
  </>,

lines: + * High-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons with multi-material fills.
- * Designed for display contexts (empty states, library tiles, mode splash areas)
- * where a 48px render size rewards material hatching, fills, and architectural detail.
+ * Each icon uses two or more material zones so the Color variant
- * Color scheme — all fills are driven by CSS custom properties so callers can
- * switch between variants by setting variables on a wrapper element:
+ * CSS custom properties (set on a wrapper element):
+ *   --hi-leaf                          — timber: door leaf, window frame, fascia
+ * Without variables the icons render in monochrome currentColor.
- * When the variables are not set the icons render in a tasteful monochrome
- * using currentColor at low opacity (default behaviour, theme-adaptive).
-// ── Wall: plan cross-section with concrete hatch ──────────────────────────────
+// ── Wall: 3-layer plan section — finish | concrete core | finish ──────────────
+    <line x1="8"  y1="18" x2="8"  y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
+    <line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
-// ── Door: plan view — wall with opening, hinge, door leaf, and swing arc ──────
+// ── Door: concrete walls + wood jamb liners + door leaf + swing arc ───────────
-    {/* Door leaf — thin panel in open (perpendicular) position */}
-    <rect x="14.5" y="24" width="3" height="16" style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} />
-// ── Window: plan view — wall with reveal, frame, and glass panes ──────────────
+// ── Window: concrete walls + wood frame sections + double-pane glass ──────────
+    {/* Wood frame — outer section (y=14..21) and inner section (y=27..34) */}
-    {/* Glass: double pane with fill between */}
-// ── Stair: plan view — 4 treads, nosing lines, cut diagonal, up-arrow ─────────
+// ── Stair: plan view — concrete body + tread finish strips + arrow ─────────────
<line x1="10" y1="10" x2="42" y2="42" strokeWidth={1.5} strokeDasharray="4 3" />
-// ── Column: plan cross-section — concrete hatch, bold border, grid lines ──────
+// ── Column: plan section — concrete with steel rebar at corners ───────────────
-    <line x1="24" y1="4" x2="24" y2="14" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
+    <line x1="24" y1="4"  x2="24" y2="14" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
<line x1="24" y1="34" x2="24" y2="41" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
-    <line x1="4" y1="24" x2="14" y2="24" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />

<line x1="34" y1="24" x2="44" y2="24" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray="3 2" />
-// ── Beam: I-section cross-section — flanges, web, steel fill, fillet radii ────
+// ── Beam: composite — concrete slab on steel I-section ────────────────────────
+    <rect x="4" y="4" width="40" height="10" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />
+    <rect x="8" y="14" width="32" height="7" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />
-    {/* Fillet radii at top flange–web junctions */}
+    <rect x="8" y="33" width="32" height="7" strokeWidth={1.5} fill="none" />
-    {/* Fillet radii at bottom flange–web junctions */}
-// ── Roof: pitched elevation — ridge, two slopes, tile courses, eave ───────────
+// ── Roof: tile surface + ridge beam + wood fascia ─────────────────────────────
-// ── Room: plan view — dashed boundary, interior fill, centroid cross ──────────
+// ── Room: plan view — concrete wall bands + room interior ─────────────────────
-    {/* Interior fill — transparent in mono, pastel in color mode */}
+    {/* Wall band fills — top, bottom, left, right */}
-    <rect x="5" y="5" width="38" height="38" strokeWidth={1.5} strokeDasharray="4 3" />
-    <line x1="15" y1="33" x2="33" y2="33" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.45} />
-    <line x1="18" y1="37" x2="30" y2="37" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.45} />
+    <line x1="16" y1="33" x2="32" y2="33" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.45} />
-// ── Section: cut-line symbol — heavy dash, end bubbles, view arrows ────────────
+// ── Section: cut marker through a concrete wall ────────────────────────────────
+    {/* Concrete wall being cut — above and below the cut line */}
+    {/* Sparse hatch in upper wall zone */}
+    <line x1="14" y1="11" x2="34" y2="11" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.4} />

<line x1="14" y1="18" x2="34" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} strokeDasharray="5 3" />

<line x1="34" y1="18" x2="42" y2="18" strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.5} />

content.text:
Clean. The icons package compiles without errors. All 10 icons now have multiple material zones:

- **WallHifi** — finish face | concrete core | finish face (3 layers)
- **DoorHifi** — concrete walls + wood jamb liners + wood leaf (3 materials)
- **WindowHifi** — concrete walls + wood frame sections + glass (3 materials)
- **StairHifi** — concrete body + tread finish strips (2 materials)
- **ColumnHifi** — concrete + steel rebar dots at corners (2 materials)
- **BeamHifi** — concrete slab on top + steel I-section below (2 materials)
- **RoofHifi** — roof tiles + wood fascia board + wood ridge cap (2 materials)
- **RoomHifi** — concrete wall bands + room interior (2 materials)
- **SectionHifi** — concrete wall being cut + section marker (2 materials)
- **FloorHifi** — screed topping + structural slab (2 materials)

The new `--hi-finish` variable (`#f0ead8` warm off-white in Color mode) handles plaster faces, screed topping, and stair tread surfaces. Check `/icons` to see all three variants now showing genuine multi-material Color mode.

input.description: Create hifi-architectural.tsx with 9 icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-architectural.tsx` with 9 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx` and produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. Each icon receives `paths` as a ReactNode of SVG children. The SVG root already sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API (exact signature)

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children here */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system — MUST use these for fills

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural concrete (gray) |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | plaster, gypsum, screed (warm white) |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | glazing panes (blue) |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | steel sections, bolts, aluminum (blue-gray) |
| `--hi-leaf` | timber: door leaf, handrail, frame (amber/brown), no opacity var |
| `--hi-roof` / `--hi-roof-op` | roof surface (terracotta) |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | room interior fill (pale yellow) |

Pattern for fill zones:
```tsx
<rect ... style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
```
For `--hi-leaf` (no op var): `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5}`

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary edges, reveals
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — hatch lines, detail, text placeholder lines

## Example icon for reference (WallHifi — 3-layer plan section)

```tsx
export const WallHifi = bimIconHifi('WallHifi', <>
  <rect x="4" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <rect x="8" y="18" width="32" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
  <rect x="40" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.35}>
    <line x1="8" y1="24" x2="14" y2="18" />
    <line x1="8" y1="30" x2="20" y2="18" />
    <line x1="14" y1="30" x2="26" y2="18" />
    <line x1="20" y1="30" x2="32" y2="18" />
    <line x1="26" y1="30" x2="38" y2="18" />
  </g>
  <line x1="8" y1="18" x2="8" y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
  <line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="30" strokeWidth={1} strokeOpacity={0.5} />
  <line x1="4" y1="18" x2="44" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
  <line x1="4" y1="30" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={2} />
  <line x1="4" y1="18" x2="4" y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
  <line x1="44" y1="18" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={1.5} />
</>);
```

## Icons to create (all plan or elevation views, leave ~4px margins from canvas edge)

### 1. RailingHifi
Plan view of a railing system. Show two parallel long rails (top rail `--hi-steel`) running horizontally across the icon, with 4–5 vertical baluster posts between them (`--hi-leaf` for wood posts). The outer rail should be bold (strokeWidth 2), the inner rail 1.5, the balusters 1. Add a mounting base plate fill (`--hi-steel`) at each end.

### 2. CurtainWallHifi
Plan view cross-section of a curtain wall. Left and right wall stubs in `--hi-concrete` (hatched). In the opening: three glass panes (`--hi-glass` fill, thin rects) separated by thin aluminum mullions (`--hi-steel` — just thin lines at 1px). The glass panes fill the full wall depth.

### 3. CeilingHifi
Reflected ceiling plan. Show a rectangular ceiling boundary (strokeWidth 2). Inside, draw a grid of ceiling tiles using thin lines (2×3 grid of tiles, strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.5). In one tile, add a simple recessed light fixture (small square with cross lines). Ceiling fill: `--hi-finish`. The tile grid lines should be strokeOpacity 0.4.

### 4. OpeningHifi
Plan view of an opening in a wall. Left wall stub and right wall stub in `--hi-concrete` (hatched). The opening between them has NO fill (void). Show the opening extent with small perpendicular tick marks at each jamb edge (similar to a door jamb but no door). Add a dashed rectangle above/below the opening showing the opening boundary extents.

### 5. RampHifi
Plan view of a ramp. Rectangular boundary. Fill with `--hi-finish` (floor finish). Draw diagonal parallel lines across the rect to indicate slope (like contour lines at 45°, strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.4). At one end draw an up-arrow (direction of incline). At the bottom edge draw two short vertical lines showing the ramp edge/nosing in strokeWidth 2.

### 6. PartitionHifi
Plan view of a partition (drywall) wall — lighter than structural wall. Two thin `--hi-finish` layers on outside, thin gypsum board core in the middle (also `--hi-finish` but slightly different opacity), with a thin air/insulation gap in center (no fill). The overall wall is thinner than WallHifi (about 6–8px total height vs 12px). NO concrete hatch — this is a lightweight partition.

### 7. TagHifi
An annotation tag. Draw a rounded rectangular balloon shape (like a callout bubble) with a small triangle leader pointing down-left. Inside the balloon: two horizontal placeholder lines (strokeWidth 1, strokeOpacity 0.4) representing text. The balloon fill is `--hi-finish`. A small circle at the leader tip.

### 8. DimensionHifi
A linear dimension annotation. Two vertical extension lines on left and right. A horizontal dimension line between them with small perpendicular tick marks (or arrows) at each end. A short placeholder text box in the center of the dimension line (two horizontal lines for text, strokeWidth 0.75). Extension lines should extend slightly past the dimension line. Use `--hi-steel` for the dimension line fill (the tick/arrow zone).

### 9. MassHifi
Conceptual massing — 3D isometric box. Draw three visible faces of a box in isometric projection:
- Front face (bottom-left to bottom-right): `--hi-concrete` fill at normal opacity
- Top face (trapezoid above front): `--hi-finish` fill at slightly lower opacity  
- Side face (right of front): `--hi-concrete` at slightly lower opacity than front
Use parallelograms/polygons for the isometric faces. Bold edges (strokeWidth 2) for silhouette, medium (1.5) for shared face edges.

## Output

Write the complete file. File header should document the CSS variables. Each icon should have a short `// ── IconName ──` comment header. Export all 9 icons.

Required exports: `RailingHifi`, `CurtainWallHifi`, `CeilingHifi`, `OpeningHifi`, `RampHifi`, `PartitionHifi`, `TagHifi`, `DimensionHifi`, `MassHifi`

Plan view cross-section of a curtain wall. Left and right wall stubs in `--hi-concrete` (hatched). In the opening: three glass panes (`--hi-glass` fill, thin rects) separated by thin aluminum mullions (`--hi-steel` — just thin lines at 1px). The glass panes fill the full wall depth.

### 3. CeilingHifi
Reflected ceiling plan. Show a rectangular ceiling boundary (strokeWidth 2). Inside, draw a grid of ceiling tiles using thin lines (2×3 grid of tiles, strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.5). In one tile, add a simple recessed light fixture (small square with cross lines). Ceiling fill: `--hi-finish`. The tile grid lines should be strokeOpacity 0.4.

### 4. OpeningHifi
Plan view of an opening in a wall. Left wall stub and right wall stub in `--hi-concrete` (hatched). The opening between them has NO fill (void). Show the opening extent with small perpendicular tick marks at each jamb edge (similar to a door jamb but no door). Add a dashed rectangle above/below the opening showing the opening boundary extents.

### 5. RampHifi
Plan view of a ramp. Rectangular boundary. Fill with `--hi-finish` (floor finish). Draw diagonal parallel lines across the rect to indicate slope (like contour lines at 45°, strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.4). At one end draw an up-arrow (direction of incline). At the bottom edge draw two short vertical lines showing the ramp edge/nosing in strokeWidth 2.

### 6. PartitionHifi
Plan view of a partition (drywall) wall — lighter than structural wall. Two thin `--hi-finish` layers on outside, thin gypsum board core in the middle (also `--hi-finish` but slightly different opacity), with a thin air/insulation gap in center (no fill). The overall wall is thinner than WallHifi (about 6–8px total height vs 12px). NO concrete hatch — this is a lightweight partition.

### 7. TagHifi
An annotation tag. Draw a rounded rectangular balloon shape (like a callout bubble) with a small triangle leader pointing down-left. Inside the balloon: two horizontal placeholder lines (strokeWidth 1, strokeOpacity 0.4) representing text. The balloon fill is `--hi-finish`. A small circle at the leader tip.

### 8. DimensionHifi
A linear dimension annotation. Two vertical extension lines on left and right. A horizontal dimension line between them with small perpendicular tick marks (or arrows) at each end. A short placeholder text box in the center of the dimension line (two horizontal lines for text, strokeWidth 0.75). Extension lines should extend slightly past the dimension line. Use `--hi-steel` for the dimension line fill (the tick/arrow zone).

### 9. MassHifi
Conceptual massing — 3D isometric box. Draw three visible faces of a box in isometric projection:
- Front face (bottom-left to bottom-right): `--hi-concrete` fill at normal opacity
- Top face (trapezoid above front): `--hi-finish` fill at slightly lower opacity  
- Side face (right of front): `--hi-concrete` at slightly lower opacity than front
Use parallelograms/polygons for the isometric faces. Bold edges (strokeWidth 2) for silhouette, medium (1.5) for shared face edges.

## Output

Write the complete file. File header should document the CSS variables. Each icon should have a short `// ── IconName ──` comment header. Export all 9 icons.

Required exports: `RailingHifi`, `CurtainWallHifi`, `CeilingHifi`, `OpeningHifi`, `RampHifi`, `PartitionHifi`, `TagHifi`, `DimensionHifi`, `MassHifi`

input.description: Create hifi-mep.tsx with 8 MEP icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-mep.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM MEP icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx` and produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. The SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children here */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system — MUST use these for fills

| Variable | Purpose | Color in Color mode |
|---|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural concrete | warm stone gray |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | plaster, insulation, casing | warm off-white |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | glazing, transparent panels | sky blue |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | steel, aluminum, galvanized metal, ductwork | blue-gray |
| `--hi-leaf` | timber/organic (no opacity var) | amber-brown |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | interior zone fill | pale yellow |

Pattern:
```tsx
<rect ... style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }} />
```

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines / main duct walls
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary details
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — internal detail, hatch, insulation lines

## Reference icon (WallHifi — shows 3-layer section pattern)
```tsx
export const WallHifi = bimIconHifi('WallHifi', <>
  <rect x="4" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <rect x="8" y="18" width="32" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
  <rect x="40" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <line x1="4" y1="18" x2="44" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
  <line x1="4" y1="30" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={2} />
</>);
```

## Icons to create (leave ~4px margins from canvas edge)

Each icon MUST have at least 2 distinct fill zones using different CSS variables.

### 1. DuctRectHifi
Cross-section of a rectangular HVAC duct, shown centered in the canvas. Outer duct body: `--hi-steel` (galvanized sheet metal). Inner air cavity: `--hi-room` (the air space inside). Draw the duct as a centered rectangle with about 4px wall thickness all around. Show the steel wall as the filled zone between outer rect and inner rect (use two nested rects or a path with evenodd fill). Add short flow arrows inside showing airflow direction. Optional: show insulation as a thin outer band in `--hi-finish` wrapped around the duct exterior.

### 2. DuctRoundHifi
Cross-section of a round duct. Centered large circle for outer duct shell (`--hi-steel`). Smaller inner circle for air space (`--hi-room`). The wall thickness between them is the steel. Show 3–4 concentric rings fading out with decreasing strokeOpacity to suggest the cylindrical cross-section. Add a short horizontal arrow inside for airflow.

### 3. PipeHifi
Cross-section of a structural pipe (plumbing). Larger and heavier-walled than DuctRound. Outer circle = pipe body (`--hi-concrete` or `--hi-steel`). Inner circle = fluid void (`--hi-glass` tinted, suggesting water). Pipe wall noticeably thicker than duct. Show pipe in the upper-center of canvas, with a small cross-section hatch in the pipe wall using diagonal lines (strokeWidth 0.75).

### 4. CableTrayHifi
Cross-section of an open-top cable tray. Draw a U-channel (open at top) as the tray body: `--hi-steel`. Inside the tray, show 3–4 small circles representing cable bundles (`--hi-leaf` or `--hi-concrete`). The cable tray cross-section should be roughly centered, tray opening facing up. Add some texture lines on the tray bottom (perforated look: small dots or short dashes).

### 5. MechanicalEquipmentHifi
Elevation view of an HVAC rooftop unit. A rectangular main body (`--hi-steel`) with:
- Supply air connection: a short rectangular duct stub on the left side
- Return air connection: on the right
- Control panel / access door on the front face: rectangle with `--hi-finish` fill and a handle line
- Mounting base: a slightly wider concrete pad (`--hi-concrete`) at the bottom
- Fan grill on top: a circle with cross lines inside

### 6. PlumbingFixtureHifi
Plan view of a washbasin/sink. An oval/rounded rectangle for the basin (`--hi-finish` fill). A small circle at center for the drain. A faucet: two small circles (hot/cold taps) near the back edge with a connecting arc. The overflow: a small rectangle near top of basin. Bold outline (strokeWidth 2) for the basin edge, softer detail inside.

### 7. LightingFixtureHifi
Reflected ceiling plan of a recessed luminaire. Outer square or rectangle boundary representing the ceiling opening/trim (`--hi-steel` for the metal trim). Inner square for the lamp face (`--hi-room` or `--hi-glass` — light emitting surface). Diagonal lines from corners showing beam spread below. A small circle in center for the bulb symbol.

### 8. MepSpaceHifi
Plan view of an MEP zone/space boundary. A large rounded rectangular zone (`--hi-room` fill). Dashed or dotted boundary line. Inside: a small symbol showing air supply (arrow pointing down + small diffuser rectangle in `--hi-steel`). In one corner: a small thermostat symbol (circle). The zone boundary should feel different from a Room (more technical, less architectural).

## Output

Write the complete file with all 8 icons. File header should document the CSS variable system. Each icon gets a short comment header.

Required exports: `DuctRectHifi`, `DuctRoundHifi`, `PipeHifi`, `CableTrayHifi`, `MechanicalEquipmentHifi`, `PlumbingFixtureHifi`, `LightingFixtureHifi`, `MepSpaceHifi`

input.description: Create hifi-structural.tsx with 6 structural icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-structural.tsx` with 6 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM structural icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx` and produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. The SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children here */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system — MUST use for fills

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural concrete (warm stone gray) |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | plaster, grout, fill (warm off-white) |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | steel, rebar, bolts, plates (blue-gray) |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | interior void / air space (pale yellow) |

Pattern:
```tsx
<rect ... style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
```
Without variables set → renders in tasteful monochrome (currentColor low opacity).

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary structural outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary edges
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — hatch, detail lines

## Reference: WallHifi (3-layer plan section)
```tsx
export const WallHifi = bimIconHifi('WallHifi', <>
  <rect x="4" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <rect x="8" y="18" width="32" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }} />
  <rect x="40" y="18" width="4" height="12"
    style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-finish, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-finish-op, 0.06)' }} />
  <g strokeWidth={0.75} strokeOpacity={0.35}>
    <line x1="8" y1="24" x2="14" y2="18" />
    <line x1="8" y1="30" x2="20" y2="18" />
    <line x1="14" y1="30" x2="26" y2="18" />
  </g>
  <line x1="4" y1="18" x2="44" y2="18" strokeWidth={2} />
  <line x1="4" y1="30" x2="44" y2="30" strokeWidth={2} />
</>);
```

## Icons to create (leave ~4px canvas margin; each needs 2+ material zones)

### 1. FoundationHifi
Plan view of a pad footing with column stub. Outer foundation pad: large square (~36×36, centered) with `--hi-concrete` fill + concrete diagonal hatch inside. Column stub: smaller square (~12×12, centered) with `--hi-concrete` bold border (strokeWidth 2). The footing beyond the column is shown with thinner strokes and lower opacity fill. Dashed lines from column to footing edge showing the stepped profile.

### 2. StripFootingHifi
Cross-section (section cut) of a strip footing. Shows a wide footing base (full canvas width, about 14px tall at bottom) with `--hi-concrete` fill + hatch. Rising from it: a narrower wall (8px wide, centered, about 18px tall) also in `--hi-concrete`. The two zones (footing + wall) should have slightly different hatch directions or density to read as separate pours. Bold outlines (strokeWidth 2) for all boundaries.

### 3. TrussHifi
Elevation view of a simple Pratt truss. Top chord: horizontal line at top. Bottom chord: horizontal line at bottom. Three vertical posts. Four diagonal web members (alternating direction). All members are steel: use `--hi-steel` for the member body fills (the members shown as narrow rectangles or fat lines). The top chord has a small rectangular cross-section fill. The bottom chord similar. Bold (strokeWidth 2) for chord outlines, 1.5 for web members.

### 4. BraceHifi
Structural diagram showing an X-brace between two columns. Two vertical columns on left/right (`--hi-steel` fill). One or two diagonal braces crossing between them (`--hi-steel`). The columns should show as I-section hints (or just thick rectangles). The brace members as narrow diagonal rects. Bold connection plates at each end (`--hi-steel`, slightly different fill opacity).

### 5. RebarHifi
Cross-section of reinforced concrete. A rectangular concrete section filling most of the canvas (`--hi-concrete` with diagonal hatch). Inside: a grid of rebar circles at regular intervals — 3×3 or 2×4 arrangement — each small circle filled solid with `--hi-steel` (fillOpacity 0.85, stroke="none"). The rebar circles should be clearly visible against the hatched background. Also show a thin stirrup (rectangular loop) in `--hi-steel` outline running around the inside perimeter.

### 6. StructuralConnectionHifi
Bolt plate splice connection. Two steel base plates partially overlapping (`--hi-steel` fill). At the overlap zone: 4–6 bolt circles arranged in a pattern (small filled circles, `--hi-steel`). The plates extend left and right to show they're connecting structural elements. Weld lines at plate edges (short perpendicular tick marks at intervals, strokeWidth 0.75). The connecting element stubs (flanges) visible at each end in `--hi-steel`.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 6 icons. Include a file header documenting the CSS variables. Each icon has a short comment header.

Required exports: `FoundationHifi`, `StripFootingHifi`, `TrussHifi`, `BraceHifi`, `RebarHifi`, `StructuralConnectionHifi`

Structural diagram showing an X-brace between two columns. Two vertical columns on left/right (`--hi-steel` fill). One or two diagonal braces crossing between them (`--hi-steel`). The columns should show as I-section hints (or just thick rectangles). The brace members as narrow diagonal rects. Bold connection plates at each end (`--hi-steel`, slightly different fill opacity).

### 5. RebarHifi
Cross-section of reinforced concrete. A rectangular concrete section filling most of the canvas (`--hi-concrete` with diagonal hatch). Inside: a grid of rebar circles at regular intervals — 3×3 or 2×4 arrangement — each small circle filled solid with `--hi-steel` (fillOpacity 0.85, stroke="none"). The rebar circles should be clearly visible against the hatched background. Also show a thin stirrup (rectangular loop) in `--hi-steel` outline running around the inside perimeter.

### 6. StructuralConnectionHifi
Bolt plate splice connection. Two steel base plates partially overlapping (`--hi-steel` fill). At the overlap zone: 4–6 bolt circles arranged in a pattern (small filled circles, `--hi-steel`). The plates extend left and right to show they're connecting structural elements. Weld lines at plate edges (short perpendicular tick marks at intervals, strokeWidth 0.75). The connecting element stubs (flanges) visible at each end in `--hi-steel`.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 6 icons. Include a file header documenting the CSS variables. Each icon has a short comment header.

Required exports: `FoundationHifi`, `StripFootingHifi`, `TrussHifi`, `BraceHifi`, `RebarHifi`, `StructuralConnectionHifi`

input.description: Create hifi-views.tsx with 8 view icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-views.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM view icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children here */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system

| Variable | Purpose | Color in Color mode |
|---|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | walls, structural | warm stone gray |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | paper, white, plaster | warm off-white |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | glazing | sky blue |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | steel, metal, header rows | blue-gray |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | interior space, data rows | pale yellow |

Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}`

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — hatch, grid lines, text placeholder lines

## Icons to create (48×48 viewBox, ~4px margin from edges; each needs 2+ fill zones)

### 1. PlanViewHifi
A miniature schematic floor plan. Show walls as thick lines (3–4px stroke, or as narrow filled rects in `--hi-concrete`). Draw a simple L-shaped or rectangular room layout with:
- Room interior fill: `--hi-room`
- One door swing in one corner (quarter-circle arc + door leaf line)
- A window opening (double line) in one wall
The plan should feel like a real tiny floor plan, not an abstract symbol.

### 2. SectionViewHifi
A section/elevation showing a building cut. Left side: a wall in section (concrete hatched, `--hi-concrete`). Right side: open interior view (room fill `--hi-room`). At bottom: a floor slab section (`--hi-concrete` thin horizontal band). A bold vertical cut line on the left. Small view direction triangle/arrow pointing right. Optional: a window opening in the wall.

### 3. ElevationViewHifi
Building elevation view (exterior face). A rectangular building outline (`--hi-finish` for wall face). Grid of windows: 2 columns × 2 rows, each window with glass fill (`--hi-glass`). Horizontal band at bottom for plinth/base (`--hi-concrete`). Small elevation level marker (horizontal line with tick) at left edge. Bold outline (strokeWidth 2).

### 4. SheetHifi
A drawing sheet. Outer border = sheet boundary (bold, strokeWidth 2). Inner border offset by 4px = drawing margin (strokeWidth 1). Lower strip (bottom 10px) = title block zone (`--hi-steel` light fill). Inside the main area: a smaller rectangle representing a placed view/viewport (dashed border, `--hi-finish` fill). Sheet background: `--hi-finish` (paper white).

### 5. ScheduleViewHifi
A BIM schedule (table). Draw 4 rows × 3 columns grid. Header row: `--hi-steel` fill with bold bottom border. Alternating data rows: odd rows `--hi-room` light fill, even rows no fill. Vertical column dividers (strokeWidth 0.75). Each cell has a short horizontal placeholder line for text (strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.4). Bold outer border (strokeWidth 2).

### 6. CalloutHifi
A callout annotation. A rectangular callout bubble (main body, `--hi-finish` fill, rounded corners if possible). A triangular leader extending from one corner of the rectangle toward a target point. Inside the bubble: two text placeholder lines. Outside the bubble near the target: a dashed rectangular 'detail boundary' showing the area being called out.

### 7. GridLineHifi
Structural grid intersection. Two perpendicular grid lines (one horizontal, one vertical) crossing at canvas center. Each line extends to a circle bubble at its far end. The circles (r≈5) are shown with `--hi-steel` fill at low opacity. The lines are strokeDasharray (long-dash pattern). Inside each circle bubble: a small horizontal placeholder line for the grid label.

### 8. LevelHifi
A level datum marker. A long horizontal datum line crossing the full canvas width at mid-height. At the left end: a level bubble/marker — a circle or diamond shape with `--hi-steel` fill. A short vertical line drops from the datum to a base marker. At the right: another level indicator. Small text placeholder lines for the elevation value. An arrowhead pointing up above the datum line.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 8 icons. Include a header comment documenting the CSS variables. Each icon has a short comment header.

Required exports: `PlanViewHifi`, `SectionViewHifi`, `ElevationViewHifi`, `SheetHifi`, `ScheduleViewHifi`, `CalloutHifi`, `GridLineHifi`, `LevelHifi`

input.description: Create hifi-organization.tsx with 8 organization icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-organization.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM organization/coordination icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx`, produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. The SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system

| Variable | Purpose | Color mode value |
|---|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural concrete | stone gray |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | paper, plaster, background | warm off-white |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | glazing, transparent | sky blue |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | steel, metal, frames | blue-gray |
| `--hi-leaf` | timber, wood tones (no op var) | amber-brown |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | interior zone | pale yellow |
| `--hi-roof` / `--hi-roof-op` | roof, alert/issue color | terracotta red |

Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }}`
For `--hi-leaf`: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.45}`

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — detail, text placeholder

## Icons to create (48×48, ~4px margins; each needs 2+ fill zones)

### 1. FamilyHifi
A BIM family — the parametric template concept. Draw a window/door element with visible parameter dimension arrows:
- The element outline (simple window: wall section + glazing in `--hi-glass`)
- Dimension arrows on each side with text placeholder lines (like a parametric drawing)
- Small "W=" and "H=" text placeholder lines at edges (short horizontal lines in strokeOpacity 0.4)
- Parameter grip handles: small square dots at corners in `--hi-steel`

### 2. FamilyTypeHifi
Three type variants. Draw three progressively different window configurations side by side (small, medium, wide), each a tiny rectangle with `--hi-glass` fill inside a wall stub frame. Connect them with a thin horizontal bracket above. A small "A / B / C" type indicator (three short horizontal lines at different widths below each).

### 3. GroupHifi
A group selection. Draw 3–4 simplified BIM elements inside (a short wall segment, a door rectangle, a window opening — simplified as geometric shapes). Around all of them: a rounded dashed selection boundary (`--hi-steel` stroke, strokeDasharray "5 3"). The interior elements each have their own small fill. A small group icon symbol (chain links or brackets) in one corner.

### 4. LinkedModelHifi
Two chain links connecting two BIM models. Draw two overlapping ovals/links (the chain link symbol) in `--hi-steel`. Behind them: two small building floor plan outlines (tiny rectangles with room fills in `--hi-room`), one on each side. The link connects them visually in the center.

### 5. MaterialHifi
A material sample swatch. Draw a layered stack of 4 material bands, each about 10px tall, stacked vertically, full canvas width:
- Top: exterior render / paint — `--hi-finish`
- Second: concrete structural — `--hi-concrete` with sparse hatch
- Third: insulation — `--hi-finish` at different opacity, with small wavy lines (strokeWidth 0.75) to suggest insulation batt
- Bottom: interior gypsum — `--hi-finish` different opacity
Each layer has a bold horizontal boundary line. The bands should clearly show different materials.

### 6. PhaseHifi
Construction phase timeline. Draw a horizontal timeline bar spanning the canvas. Show two phase brackets above the bar (Phase 1 bracket on left half, Phase 2 bracket on right half). Phase 1 fill: `--hi-concrete`. Phase 2 fill: `--hi-steel`. A vertical cursor line at the current date. Small milestone diamonds on the timeline. Text placeholder lines below each phase.

### 7. IssueHifi
A location-based issue/pin. Draw a map pin shape (circle on top, pointed bottom) as the main body, fill with `--hi-roof` (terracotta/red for alert). Inside the circle: a small exclamation mark or priority indicator (horizontal lines). Below the pin tip: a small plan context (dashed room outline in `--hi-room` showing the pin's location in a space). The pin should dominate the icon.

### 8. ClashHifi
Two structural elements in collision. Left element: a wall plan cross-section in `--hi-concrete`. Right element: a pipe or duct cross-section in `--hi-steel`. They overlap in the center. The overlap/clash zone: filled with `--hi-roof` (red/terracotta) at moderate opacity to highlight the conflict. A small starburst or X marks at the collision center.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 8 icons. Include a header comment. Each icon has a short `// ── IconName ──` comment header.

Required exports: `FamilyHifi`, `FamilyTypeHifi`, `GroupHifi`, `LinkedModelHifi`, `MaterialHifi`, `PhaseHifi`, `IssueHifi`, `ClashHifi`

input.description: Create hifi-site-annotation.tsx with 8 site/annotation icons
input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-site-annotation.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM site and annotation icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx`, produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. The SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system

| Variable | Purpose | Color mode |
|---|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural, terrain | stone gray |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | paper, annotation fill | warm off-white |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | water, transparent zones | sky blue |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | metal markers, frames | blue-gray |
| `--hi-leaf` | vegetation, grass (no op var) | amber-brown (use for plants) |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | site areas, zones | pale yellow |
| `--hi-roof` / `--hi-roof-op` | boundaries, alerts | terracotta red |

Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-room, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-room-op, 0.07)' }}`
For `--hi-leaf`: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5}`

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — detail, text, annotation lines

## Icons to create (48×48, ~4px margins; each needs 2+ fill zones)

### 1. TopoHifi
Topographic contour map. Draw 5–6 concentric irregular closed curves (ellipses slightly offset from center, getting smaller toward the top). The curves represent elevation contours. Fill the innermost zone (hilltop) with `--hi-concrete` at moderate opacity, the middle zone with `--hi-room`, outer zone with `--hi-glass` lightly (representing lower ground/water). Each contour line at strokeWidth 1. Small elevation text placeholder lines at the contours (short dashes).

### 2. PropertyLineHifi
A property/site boundary. Draw a quadrilateral (irregular pentagon or quadrilateral) representing a site parcel. The boundary lines are bold (strokeWidth 2). At two corners: small corner marker symbols (T-shaped or L-shaped survey markers, `--hi-steel` fill). Inside the parcel: `--hi-room` fill. Along one boundary edge: a short bearing annotation (short perpendicular tick + placeholder angle text line). A small pin/stake marker on one corner.

### 3. NorthArrowHifi
Architectural north arrow / compass rose. Large arrow pointing up (north) with a distinctive architectural style. The north half of the arrow head: solid `--hi-steel` fill. The south half: `--hi-finish`. A circle ring around the arrow base. "N" text placeholder at arrow tip (two short lines forming N shape). Small tick marks at 90° intervals (E, S, W). Elegant and simple.

### 4. RevisionCloudHifi
A revision cloud annotation marking a changed area. Draw a closed path made of ~8 small arc bumps (semicircles) forming a cloud outline around a rectangular zone. Inside the cloud: `--hi-finish` fill at low opacity. The cloud bumps are the main visual element (each bump is a small arc, strokeWidth 1.5). Inside the cloud: a small revision triangle/delta symbol at one corner in `--hi-steel`. Optional: short placeholder text lines for the revision number.

### 5. ScaleBarHifi
A graphical scale bar. Draw a horizontal bar divided into 5 equal segments (alternating filled/unfilled). Filled segments: `--hi-steel` or `--hi-concrete` fill. Unfilled: `--hi-finish`. Bold outline of the full bar. Short vertical tick marks at each division. Text placeholder lines below each division for the measurement. End markers with "0" placeholder at left, "5m" placeholder at right.

### 6. AreaLabelHifi
An area annotation label. Draw a large tag/badge shape (rounded rectangle or hexagon) as the main body (`--hi-finish` fill, bold border). Inside: two text placeholder zones — one larger line at top (room name) and one smaller below (area value like "24.5 m²"). A small area-boundary indicator: dashed lines extending from the tag corners suggesting the measured space. Optional leader line from tag to space interior.

### 7. KeynoteHifi
A keynote annotation balloon. Draw a hexagonal or circular balloon (`--hi-steel` fill at low opacity for the balloon). Inside: a number placeholder (short horizontal bars). A leader line extending diagonally from the balloon to a target point. At the target: a small filled circle. The balloon should be bold-outlined (strokeWidth 2) and prominent. This is used in construction documents to reference keynote descriptions.

### 8. SpotElevationHifi
A spot elevation annotation. Draw an upward-pointing triangle/arrow (the datum marker) with `--hi-steel` fill. From the tip: a short horizontal datum line extending left. Above the datum line: text placeholder lines for the elevation value (e.g., "+3.200"). Below: ground/floor line. A small circle at the intersection. Bold and clean — this appears frequently on architectural drawings.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 8 icons. Include a header comment. Each icon has a `// ── IconName ──` comment header.

Required exports: `TopoHifi`, `PropertyLineHifi`, `NorthArrowHifi`, `RevisionCloudHifi`, `ScaleBarHifi`, `AreaLabelHifi`, `KeynoteHifi`, `SpotElevationHifi`

toolUseResult.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-site-annotation.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM site and annotation icons. This is a NEW file — use the Write tool to create it.

## Context

The icon library lives at `packages/icons/src/`. The `bimIconHifi` factory is in `./icon.tsx`, produces SVG components with a 48×48 viewBox. The SVG root sets `fill="none"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeLinecap="round"`, `strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## bimIconHifi API

```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children */}
</>);
```

## CSS variable system

| Variable | Purpose | Color mode |
|---|---|---|
| `--hi-concrete` / `--hi-concrete-op` | structural, terrain | stone gray |
| `--hi-finish` / `--hi-finish-op` | paper, annotation fill | warm off-white |
| `--hi-glass` / `--hi-glass-op` | water, transparent zones | sky blue |
| `--hi-steel` / `--hi-steel-op` | metal markers, frames | blue-gray |
| `--hi-leaf` | vegetation, grass (no op var) | amber-brown (use for plants) |
| `--hi-room` / `--hi-room-op` | site areas, zones | pale yellow |
| `--hi-roof` / `--hi-roof-op` | boundaries, alerts | terracotta red |

Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-room, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-room-op, 0.07)' }}`
For `--hi-leaf`: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-leaf, currentColor)' }} fillOpacity={0.5}`

## Stroke hierarchy
- `strokeWidth={2}` — primary outlines
- `strokeWidth={1.5}` — secondary
- `strokeWidth={0.75}` — detail, text, annotation lines

## Icons to create (48×48, ~4px margins; each needs 2+ fill zones)

### 1. TopoHifi
Topographic contour map. Draw 5–6 concentric irregular closed curves (ellipses slightly offset from center, getting smaller toward the top). The curves represent elevation contours. Fill the innermost zone (hilltop) with `--hi-concrete` at moderate opacity, the middle zone with `--hi-room`, outer zone with `--hi-glass` lightly (representing lower ground/water). Each contour line at strokeWidth 1. Small elevation text placeholder lines at the contours (short dashes).

### 2. PropertyLineHifi
A property/site boundary. Draw a quadrilateral (irregular pentagon or quadrilateral) representing a site parcel. The boundary lines are bold (strokeWidth 2). At two corners: small corner marker symbols (T-shaped or L-shaped survey markers, `--hi-steel` fill). Inside the parcel: `--hi-room` fill. Along one boundary edge: a short bearing annotation (short perpendicular tick + placeholder angle text line). A small pin/stake marker on one corner.

### 3. NorthArrowHifi
Architectural north arrow / compass rose. Large arrow pointing up (north) with a distinctive architectural style. The north half of the arrow head: solid `--hi-steel` fill. The south half: `--hi-finish`. A circle ring around the arrow base. "N" text placeholder at arrow tip (two short lines forming N shape). Small tick marks at 90° intervals (E, S, W). Elegant and simple.

### 4. RevisionCloudHifi
A revision cloud annotation marking a changed area. Draw a closed path made of ~8 small arc bumps (semicircles) forming a cloud outline around a rectangular zone. Inside the cloud: `--hi-finish` fill at low opacity. The cloud bumps are the main visual element (each bump is a small arc, strokeWidth 1.5). Inside the cloud: a small revision triangle/delta symbol at one corner in `--hi-steel`. Optional: short placeholder text lines for the revision number.

### 5. ScaleBarHifi
A graphical scale bar. Draw a horizontal bar divided into 5 equal segments (alternating filled/unfilled). Filled segments: `--hi-steel` or `--hi-concrete` fill. Unfilled: `--hi-finish`. Bold outline of the full bar. Short vertical tick marks at each division. Text placeholder lines below each division for the measurement. End markers with "0" placeholder at left, "5m" placeholder at right.

### 6. AreaLabelHifi
An area annotation label. Draw a large tag/badge shape (rounded rectangle or hexagon) as the main body (`--hi-finish` fill, bold border). Inside: two text placeholder zones — one larger line at top (room name) and one smaller below (area value like "24.5 m²"). A small area-boundary indicator: dashed lines extending from the tag corners suggesting the measured space. Optional leader line from tag to space interior.

### 7. KeynoteHifi
A keynote annotation balloon. Draw a hexagonal or circular balloon (`--hi-steel` fill at low opacity for the balloon). Inside: a number placeholder (short horizontal bars). A leader line extending diagonally from the balloon to a target point. At the target: a small filled circle. The balloon should be bold-outlined (strokeWidth 2) and prominent. This is used in construction documents to reference keynote descriptions.

### 8. SpotElevationHifi
A spot elevation annotation. Draw an upward-pointing triangle/arrow (the datum marker) with `--hi-steel` fill. From the tip: a short horizontal datum line extending left. Above the datum line: text placeholder lines for the elevation value (e.g., "+3.200"). Below: ground/floor line. A small circle at the intersection. Bold and clean — this appears frequently on architectural drawings.

## Output

Write the complete file with all 8 icons. Include a header comment. Each icon has a `// ── IconName ──` comment header.

Required exports: `TopoHifi`, `PropertyLineHifi`, `NorthArrowHifi`, `RevisionCloudHifi`, `ScaleBarHifi`, `AreaLabelHifi`, `KeynoteHifi`, `SpotElevationHifi`

The complete new hifi import should be: ```ts import { WallHifi, DoorHifi, WindowHifi, StairHifi, ColumnHifi, BeamHifi, RoofHifi, RoomHifi, SectionHifi, FloorHifi, RailingHifi, CurtainWallHifi, CeilingHifi, OpeningHifi, RampHifi, PartitionHifi, TagHifi, DimensionHifi, MassHifi, DuctRectHifi, DuctRoundHifi, PipeHifi, CableTrayHifi, MechanicalEquipmentHifi, PlumbingFixtureHifi, LightingFixtureHifi, MepSpaceHifi, FoundationHifi, StripFootingHifi, TrussHifi, BraceHifi, RebarHifi, StructuralConnectionHifi, PlanViewHifi, SectionViewHifi, ElevationViewHifi, SheetHifi, ScheduleViewHifi, CalloutHifi, GridLineHifi, LevelHifi, FamilyHifi, FamilyTypeHifi, GroupHifi, LinkedModelHifi, MaterialHifi, PhaseHifi, IssueHifi, ClashHifi, TopoHifi, PropertyLineHifi, NorthArrowHifi, RevisionCloudHifi, ScaleBarHifi, AreaLabelHifi, KeynoteHifi, SpotElevationHifi, } from '@bim-ai/icons'; ``` ### B. Replace `HIFI_ICONS` flat array with `HIFI_SECTIONS` categorized structure Replace the current `const HIFI_ICONS: HiFiEntry[] = [...]` with: ```ts type HiFiSection = { label: string; icons: HiFiEntry[] }; const HIFI_SECTIONS: HiFiSection[] = [ { label: 'Architecture', icons: [ { name: 'Wall', export: 'WallHifi', Icon: WallHifi }, { name: 'Door', export: 'DoorHifi', Icon: DoorHifi }, { IssueHifi }, { name: 'Clash', export: 'ClashHifi', Icon: ClashHifi }, ], }, { label: 'Site & Annotation', icons: [ { name: 'Topography', export: 'TopoHifi', Icon: TopoHifi }, { name: 'Property line', export: 'PropertyLineHifi', Icon: PropertyLineHifi }, { name: 'North arrow', export: 'NorthArrowHifi', Icon: NorthArrowHifi }, { name: 'Revision cloud', export: 'RevisionCloudHifi', Icon: Icon: KeynoteHifi }, { name: 'Spot elevation', export: 'SpotElevationHifi', Icon: SpotElevationHifi }, ], }, ]; // Keep a flat array for total count const ALL_HIFI_ICONS = HIFI_SECTIONS.flatMap((s) => s.icons); ``` ### C.
