Replace that inner section so each color variant column shows HIFI_SECTIONS with category labels: The inner content of each variant column (inside the `style={vars}` wrapper) should become: ```tsx
{HIFI_SECTIONS.map((section) => (

{section.label}

{section.icons.map((entry) => ( ))}
))}
``` Remove the `style={vars}` from the outer `div` and put it on the new inner `div` instead (or just move it to the correct location so it wraps all icons). ### D. Update the header count The header currently shows the count of regular 24px icons. Update it to show both: - `{totalCount} 24px icons` for regular icons - `{ALL_HIFI_ICONS.length} hi-fi icons` somewhere nearby Or just keep showing `totalCount` and add a separate line for hifi count. ### E. Remove the `HIFI_ICONS` references Make sure to update ALL references from `HIFI_ICONS` → `ALL_HIFI_ICONS` or `HIFI_SECTIONS` as appropriate. There should be no remaining `HIFI_ICONS` references after this change. --- ## Important constraints - Read both files before editing - The new hifi-*.tsx files don't exist yet (being created in parallel) — that's OK, TypeScript will compile once all files are ready - Preserve ALL existing regular icon sections (SECTIONS array, IconCard component, search, etc.) exactly as-is - Only change the hifi-related parts and the imports - Use Edit tool (not Write) since both files already exist

input.description: hifi-misc-a.tsx: 8 missing Views/Org icons
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-misc-a.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
export const FooHifi = bimIconHifi('FooHifi', <>
  {/* SVG children — 48×48 viewBox, ~4px margin from edges */}
</>);
```
SVG root already sets `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`.

## CSS variable fills — use these on every fill zone
```
--hi-concrete/--hi-concrete-op   structural concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/--hi-finish-op       plaster, paper, gypsum (warm white)
--hi-glass/--hi-glass-op         glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/--hi-steel-op         steel, metal, frames (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf                        timber/wood, no opacity var
--hi-room/--hi-room-op           interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/--hi-roof-op           roof surface / alert color (terracotta)
```
Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-steel, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-steel-op, 0.12)' }}`
Each icon MUST have 2+ distinct fill zones.

Stroke: 2=primary outlines, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=hatch/detail.

## Icons

### OrbitViewHifi
3D orbit/perspective view symbol. Draw a simplified 3D box in isometric (like MassHifi): front face `--hi-room`, top face `--hi-finish`, right face `--hi-concrete` at lower opacity. Around the box: a circular orbit arc (dashed) with an arrowhead at the end. A small eye/camera symbol near the orbit path.

### ViewpointHifi
A saved camera viewpoint. Draw a camera body rectangle (`--hi-steel`) with a lens circle in front (`--hi-glass`). A small viewfinder triangle/frustum extending from the lens. Below the camera: a tiny floor plan context (`--hi-concrete` wall lines) to show the camera's position in the model.

### SectionBoxHifi
A section box (3D clipping box). Draw a 3D isometric box outline (wire frame), where three visible faces have different low-opacity fills (`--hi-concrete`, `--hi-finish`, `--hi-room`). The box has a bold outline (strokeWidth 2) and the interior is slightly lighter. Small cut arrows on each face indicating clipping direction.

### DetailLineHifi
A detail/model line annotation. A bold horizontal line (`--hi-steel` fill for the line body shown as a thin rect). At each end: small perpendicular end caps. Mid-line: a small dimension-style annotation (two short vertical lines + horizontal span). Below the line: two text placeholder lines (strokeWidth 0.75, strokeOpacity 0.4) for the line's label.

### AssemblyHifi
A structural assembly grouping. Show 3–4 different structural elements (column stub, beam stub, connection plate) arranged together, each in their material fill (`--hi-concrete` for column, `--hi-steel` for beam/plate). A dotted boundary around the whole group. A small assembly ID badge (circle + placeholder lines) in the top corner.

### WallLayerHifi
Wall layer structure — an exploded view of wall layers. Draw 4 horizontal bands (the wall in exploded cross-section), slightly offset from each other to show separation: outer layer `--hi-finish`, structural layer `--hi-concrete` (with hatch), insulation `--hi-finish` (wavy lines), inner layer `--hi-finish`. Small arrows showing the exploded direction.

### ValidationRuleHifi
A validation/QA check rule. Draw a checklist document: `--hi-finish` paper rectangle with `--hi-steel` header band. Inside: 3 checklist rows — first row has a checkmark ✓ (small path), second row has an X mark, third row has a warning ⚠ symbol. Each row separated by thin lines (0.75).

### DeviationHifi
A deviation/comparison indicator. Draw two overlapping wall sections (same wall in two different states): original position in `--hi-concrete`, deviated position slightly offset in `--hi-roof` (terracotta red). An arrow between them showing the deviation magnitude. A small delta (Δ) symbol. The overlap zone shows both fills.

## Output
Required exports: `OrbitViewHifi`, `ViewpointHifi`, `SectionBoxHifi`, `DetailLineHifi`, `AssemblyHifi`, `WallLayerHifi`, `ValidationRuleHifi`, `DeviationHifi`

```
--hi-roof/op       roof / alert (terracotta)
```

## Icons

### ElectricalPanelHifi

### FireSprinklerHifi

### DiffuserHifi
HVAC air diffuser, RCP plan view. A centered rectangle for the diffuser body (`--hi-steel`). Inside: 3–4 concentric rectangular slots with `--hi-room` fill (the air slots). Diagonal lines radiating from center to suggest airflow distribution. Bold outer frame (strokeWidth 2).

### SkyLightHifi
A skylight in a roof. Elevation cross-section view. The roof slope shown (`--hi-roof` fill) with a cutout opening. In the opening: a glass unit (`--hi-glass` fill, double pane). input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-misc-c.tsx` with 8 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox, SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`, ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op    plaster/paper (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing/water (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber/vegetation (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior/zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      roof/alert (terracotta)
```
2+ fill zones required. Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons

### ParkingSpaceHifi
Plan view of a parking space. A rectangle (one parking bay, ~30×40px) outlined boldly (strokeWidth 2). Interior: `--hi-finish` fill (pavement marking). Bold center line or stall lines (strokeWidth 1.5). A simplified car silhouette inside: two small circles (wheels) + body rectangle in `--hi-concrete` at low opacity. Dashed line at the entry end.

### PlantingHifi
A planting symbol (landscape plan). A central trunk circle (`--hi-leaf` fill). Around it: 6–8 radiating petal shapes or a star-burst outline suggesting foliage canopy. The canopy: `--hi-leaf` fill at low opacity with thin edge lines. Alternating filled/outline petals. A small shadow circle below the canopy (strokeOpacity 0.2).

### RoadHifi
A road section plan view. Two parallel bold lines (road edges, strokeWidth 2). Inside: `--hi-concrete` fill for the pavement. A dashed center line (strokeDasharray "8 4"). Small shoulder/curb strips at each edge: `--hi-finish`. Road marking arrows (straight-ahead) in the travel lane.

### RetainingWallHifi
Cross-section of a retaining wall. Left side: soil/earth fill (`--hi-room` at moderate opacity + diagonal hatch suggesting earth). Right side: the retained space (void/room). The wall itself: `--hi-concrete` rectangle with diagonal hatch, bold outline (strokeWidth 2). Foundation at base: slightly wider `--hi-concrete` block. A small drainage pipe symbol at the wall base.

### BreakLineHifi
An architectural break line / interruption symbol. A horizontal line with a distinctive zigzag in the middle (the conventional break symbol — one or two zig-zag peaks interrupting a straight line). The line ends: bold with small perpendicular terminators. The zig-zag is prominent (3–4 sharp peaks). `--hi-steel` for the line body fill zone (thin filled rect). Clean and schematic.

### CentreLineHifi
A centre line annotation (chain-dash line). Draw a long line using a chain-dash pattern (long dash, short gap, dot, short gap, repeat). At each end: a small circle endpoint. In the middle: the centre-line cross symbol (a small diamond or plus). `--hi-steel` fill for the line path/zones. Text placeholder lines for the label.

### MatchLineHifi
A match line (where two drawing sheets join). A full-width bold horizontal line across the canvas. At each end: a perpendicular T-terminus (or end cap). Above the line: "Sheet 1" placeholder (two short horizontal lines, strokeOpacity 0.4). Below: "Sheet 2" placeholder. The match line itself: bold (strokeWidth 2), with a `--hi-steel` fill zone (thin rect body). Small triangular pointers at each end.

### SlopeArrowHifi
A slope arrow annotation. A long diagonal arrow pointing uphill (from lower-left to upper-right). The arrow shaft: bold. At the tail: a filled circle indicating the start point with elevation. At the head: arrowhead. Along the shaft: small elevation markers (short perpendicular ticks). Fill zone: `--hi-steel` for the arrow body (thin rect). Text placeholder for the slope percentage.

## Output
Required exports: `ParkingSpaceHifi`, `PlantingHifi`, `RoadHifi`, `RetainingWallHifi`, `BreakLineHifi`, `CentreLineHifi`, `MatchLineHifi`, `SlopeArrowHifi`

input.description: hifi-workflow.tsx: 14 workflow/coordination icons
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-workflow.tsx` with 14 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op    paper/gypsum (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber/wood (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      roof/alert (terracotta)
```
Each icon needs 2+ fill zones. Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

For these workflow/document icons: show stylized documents/forms/data elements with material fills. Use `--hi-finish` for paper/document background, `--hi-steel` for header bands or data elements, `--hi-room` for data areas.

## Icons

### WorksetHifi
Workset (shared work partitioning). Show a circular diagram with 3 segments (like a pie chart), each segment a different fill zone (`--hi-concrete`, `--hi-steel`, `--hi-room`). Each segment represents a user's workset. A small user/person icon stub in each segment. Bold outer ring (strokeWidth 2).

### DesignOptionHifi
Design options. Show 3 small floor plan variants (A, B, C) side by side — each a tiny rectangle with different interior configurations (`--hi-room` for interiors). Above them: a bracket connecting all three. A small star/diamond on the "primary" option. The variants show simple partition walls in different arrangements.

### RevisionHifi
Revision/change tracking. A document rectangle (`--hi-finish`) with a revision cloud border (wavy/bumpy outline at the right edge). Inside: 3 rows with revision numbers (short horizontal lines). A delta (Δ) triangle symbol at top-right in `--hi-roof` (red, indicating a change). Version number badge.

### RFIHifi
Request for Information. A formal document (`--hi-finish` paper, `--hi-steel` header band). Inside the header: "RFI" text placeholder lines. Body: 3 lines of text placeholder. Bottom: response area with `--hi-room` fill. A question mark symbol prominently on the right side. Bold document border.

### SubmittalHifi
Submittal document package. A stack of documents (3 pages offset behind each other). Top document: `--hi-finish` with `--hi-steel` header and stamp mark (circle with lines inside). The stack offset shows depth. A small approval stamp symbol (circle with diagonal lines) on the top document in `--hi-roof` or `--hi-concrete`.

### PointCloudHifi
Point cloud scan data. A dense field of small dots arranged in varying density patterns — dense in center (representing scanned surfaces), sparse at edges. Use 3 density zones: inner dense (`--hi-concrete` at 0.7), mid-ring (`--hi-concrete` at 0.4), outer sparse (just dots). Dots are tiny circles (r=1) at irregular positions. Show a wall/floor silhouette emerging from the point cloud.

### DigitalTwinHifi
Digital twin / live model connection. Two overlapping rectangles: left = physical asset (`--hi-concrete`), right = digital model (`--hi-glass`). Between them: bidirectional arrows showing the sync. A small wifi/signal icon above. The overlap zone: `--hi-room` fill.

### SyncHifi
Model synchronization. A circular arrow (sync/refresh icon) composed of two arc arrows forming a circle. Inside the circle: a small model/building icon with `--hi-concrete` fill. The arrows: thick stroke with arrowheads. The ring itself: `--hi-steel` fill (for the arrow body band). A small checkmark at one arrow tip.

### TransmittalHifi
Transmittal (formal document dispatch). An envelope with a document inside. Envelope: `--hi-finish` body with `--hi-steel` flap. Inside, a document peek showing `--hi-finish` with header lines. A small "sent" arrow pointing right. Official seal/stamp suggestion on the envelope.

### IFCHifi
IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) file format. A 3D box/cube partially open (like an open container). The box: `--hi-steel`. Flowing out of it: several small geometric primitives (a circle, triangle, rectangle) representing different BIM element types in `--hi-concrete`, `--hi-room`, `--hi-glass`. This represents open BIM data interchange.

### LODHifi
Level of Detail progression. Show 3 progressively more detailed versions of the same element (e.g., a column): far-left = simple box (`--hi-concrete`, low detail), center = I-section profile, right = full detailed section with rebar dots (`--hi-steel`). Connected by small right-arrows. A progress bar at bottom showing LOD100→LOD400.

### QuantityTakeoffHifi
Quantity takeoff / BOQ. A spreadsheet/table view (`--hi-finish` background, `--hi-steel` header). Inside: 3 rows with a small icon placeholder on left (triangle, circle, rectangle) and numeric data bars on right. One row highlighted with `--hi-room` fill. A summation Σ symbol at the bottom row.

### EnergyModelHifi
Energy analysis model. A simplified building section with solar arrows hitting the roof (`--hi-roof` surface). Inside the section: color-coded energy zones (warm zone `--hi-room`, cold zone `--hi-glass`). A small sun symbol in the upper corner. Arrows indicating heat flow through the envelope.

### ScopeBoxHifi
Scope box (view range control). A 3D isometric box (dashed outline) enclosing a smaller set of building elements. The scope box boundary: bold dashed (strokeDasharray "6 3"). Inside: simplified floor plan elements (`--hi-concrete` walls, `--hi-room` interior). Corner handles on the box edges (small filled squares, `--hi-steel`).

## Output
Required exports: `WorksetHifi`, `DesignOptionHifi`, `RevisionHifi`, `RFIHifi`, `SubmittalHifi`, `PointCloudHifi`, `DigitalTwinHifi`, `SyncHifi`, `TransmittalHifi`, `IFCHifi`, `LODHifi`, `QuantityTakeoffHifi`, `EnergyModelHifi`, `ScopeBoxHifi`

Flowing out of it: several small geometric primitives (a circle, triangle, rectangle) representing different BIM element types in `--hi-concrete`, `--hi-room`, `--hi-glass`. This represents open BIM data interchange.

### LODHifi
Level of Detail progression. Show 3 progressively more detailed versions of the same element (e.g., a column): far-left = simple box (`--hi-concrete`, low detail), center = I-section profile, right = full detailed section with rebar dots (`--hi-steel`). Connected by small right-arrows. A progress bar at bottom showing LOD100→LOD400.

### QuantityTakeoffHifi
Quantity takeoff / BOQ. A spreadsheet/table view (`--hi-finish` background, `--hi-steel` header). Inside: 3 rows with a small icon placeholder on left (triangle, circle, rectangle) and numeric data bars on right. One row highlighted with `--hi-room` fill. A summation Σ symbol at the bottom row.

### EnergyModelHifi
Energy analysis model. A simplified building section with solar arrows hitting the roof (`--hi-roof` surface). Inside the section: color-coded energy zones (warm zone `--hi-room`, cold zone `--hi-glass`). A small sun symbol in the upper corner. Arrows indicating heat flow through the envelope.

### ScopeBoxHifi
Scope box (view range control). A 3D isometric box (dashed outline) enclosing a smaller set of building elements. The scope box boundary: bold dashed (strokeDasharray "6 3"). Inside: simplified floor plan elements (`--hi-concrete` walls, `--hi-room` interior). Corner handles on the box edges (small filled squares, `--hi-steel`).

## Output
Required exports: `WorksetHifi`, `DesignOptionHifi`, `RevisionHifi`, `RFIHifi`, `SubmittalHifi`, `PointCloudHifi`, `DigitalTwinHifi`, `SyncHifi`, `TransmittalHifi`, `IFCHifi`, `LODHifi`, `QuantityTakeoffHifi`, `EnergyModelHifi`, `ScopeBoxHifi`

input.description: hifi-edit-ops.tsx: 9 geometry/edit operation icons
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-edit-ops.tsx` with 9 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons for geometry/edit operations. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)  
--hi-finish/op    plaster/paper (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      roof/alert (terracotta)
```
Each icon MUST show: (1) a BIM element being operated on + (2) the operation indicator. Use 2+ fill zones.
Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons — each shows a wall/element + the operation

### MirrorHifi
A wall segment (left half of canvas) shown in `--hi-concrete` (plan section, hatched). A vertical mirror axis line in center (bold dashed). A ghost/reflected copy of the wall (right half) shown in `--hi-steel` at lower opacity (the mirrored result). A small mirror-plane indicator (double arrow perpendicular to the axis).

### ArrayLinearHifi
A small wall element (leftmost position) in `--hi-concrete`. Two more copies extending to the right, each at equal spacing, with the later copies slightly lighter (`--hi-steel` at lower opacity for "new" copies). Bidirectional arrows between elements showing the array spacing. A "×3" badge in the corner.

### ArrayRadialHifi
A central point (small circle). 4 copies of a small element (rectangle or wall stub) arranged at 90° intervals around the center. The original element in `--hi-concrete`, the arrayed copies in `--hi-steel` at lower opacity. Curved arrows between elements showing the radial arrangement.

### AlignHifi
Two wall segments with different horizontal positions (misaligned). One wall (`--hi-concrete`) is at left, another at a slightly different position (`--hi-steel`). A shared alignment target line (bold horizontal) with alignment arrows pointing from each element toward the line. The resulting "snapped" position implied.

### SplitHifi
A wall segment being split. A continuous wall (`--hi-concrete`, hatched) with a visible split point in the center (a gap or cursor mark). The two resulting wall halves are slightly separated. A small scissors or cut cursor symbol at the split point in currentColor. Bold outlines.

### TrimExtendHifi
Two wall segments: one crossing a boundary, one stopping short. The "trim" boundary is a bold diagonal line. The wall that crosses is shown being trimmed (the part beyond the boundary in `--hi-roof` at low opacity = "to be removed"). The wall stopping short shows an extend arrow toward the boundary.

### VoidHifi
A wall element (`--hi-concrete`) with a rectangular void/opening cut through it. The void shown as a `--hi-room` filled rectangle. The void edges are bold (strokeWidth 2). Dashed lines showing the void extent. Small depth arrows indicating the void goes through the full wall thickness.

### PinHifi
A wall or column element (`--hi-concrete`) with a pin symbol on it. The pin: a circle (`--hi-steel` fill) with a needle/pin extending down to the element. The element has a small "locked" indicator. The pin is prominent, centered above the element.

### UnpinHifi
Same as PinHifi but the pin is shown lifted/removed: the pin above the element with a diagonal "release" arrow or it's floating away. The element below: slightly lighter fill indicating it's now free. A small movement arrow on the element.

## Output
Required exports: `MirrorHifi`, `ArrayLinearHifi`, `ArrayRadialHifi`, `AlignHifi`, `SplitHifi`, `TrimExtendHifi`, `VoidHifi`, `PinHifi`, `UnpinHifi`

input.description: hifi-edit-basic.tsx: 14 basic modify tool icons
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-edit-basic.tsx` with 14 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons for basic modify operations. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op   concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op     plaster/paper (warm white)
--hi-glass/op      glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op      steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf          timber (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op       interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op       roof/alert (terracotta)
```
Each icon shows a BIM element being modified + the operation indicator. 2+ fill zones required.
Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons

### MoveHifi
A wall section (`--hi-concrete`) at starting position (faded/dashed outline) and at destination (solid fill). A bold arrow showing the movement from origin to destination. The ghost origin position: `--hi-steel` at low opacity.

### CopyHifi
An original wall element (`--hi-concrete`, solid) on the left. A copy of it on the right at an offset (`--hi-steel` at slightly lower opacity for the "new" copy). A dashed arrow from original to copy. Small "C" badge or copy indicator.

### RotateHifi
A wall element (`--hi-concrete`) being rotated around a center point. Show the original position and a rotated ghost position (`--hi-steel` at lower opacity). A curved arc arrow showing the rotation angle. A small rotation center marker (crosshair circle) at the pivot point.

### ScaleHifi
A wall element (`--hi-concrete`) with scale arrows extending from each corner/edge. The "before" state (smaller, `--hi-steel` at low opacity) and "after" (larger, `--hi-concrete` solid). Bold diagonal arrows from center to corners indicating uniform scale.

### OffsetHifi
A wall element (original) with an offset copy parallel to it. Original: `--hi-concrete` with hatch. Offset copy: `--hi-steel` at lower opacity. Bold perpendicular arrows between them showing the offset distance.

### DeleteHifi
A wall element (`--hi-concrete`) with a large X mark through it (two diagonal crossed lines, strokeWidth 2) in `--hi-roof` (terracotta/red). The element fill is lower opacity indicating deletion. Small deletion indicator.

### JoinGeometryHifi
Two wall segments meeting at a corner. The join point shown as a clean mitered corner. Before join: two walls with overlap shown in `--hi-roof` at low opacity. After join: clean miter in `--hi-concrete`. A small join indicator at the corner.

### UnjoinGeometryHifi
Two walls at a corner being unjoined. The walls shown with a visible separation/gap at the corner. Original join in `--hi-steel` (dashed outline), separated result with each wall ending cleanly. A break symbol at the separation.

### SwitchJoinOrderHifi
Two overlapping walls: Wall A cuts through Wall B in one configuration. Show the before state (A cuts B, shown in two materials) and a swap arrow. The "switched" state implied by the reversed priority indication.

### WallJoinsHifi
A T-junction of walls. The junction shows how walls clean up: horizontal wall (`--hi-concrete`) meeting a vertical wall (`--hi-concrete`). The intersection area shows the join cleanup (no overlap, clean miter). Dotted lines show the original unjoined extent.

### PaintMaterialHifi
A wall section (`--hi-concrete`) being painted with a new surface material. A paintbrush cursor approaching the wall face. The painted zone: `--hi-finish` fill (new paint/material). The unpainted zone: `--hi-concrete`. Clear visual division between painted and unpainted.

### MeasureBetweenHifi
Two points on a plan (two wall endpoints or corners) with a measurement line between them. The measurement line has arrowheads at each end and a text placeholder in the middle (showing "3600mm" or similar). Extension lines drop to each measurement point.

### MeasureAlongHifi
A curved or straight wall element with a measurement being taken along its length. The measurement tape/line follows the wall face. Small tick marks at regular intervals along the wall. Cumulative distance placeholder text.

### UngroupHifi
A dotted selection boundary (like GroupHifi) around several elements. The boundary is being "broken apart" — shown as the dashed line with small breaks/arrows pointing outward from it. The interior elements (wall stubs, door, etc.) are separating from each other, each with their own independent boundary hint.

## Output
Required exports: `MoveHifi`, `CopyHifi`, `RotateHifi`, `ScaleHifi`, `OffsetHifi`, `DeleteHifi`, `JoinGeometryHifi`, `UnjoinGeometryHifi`, `SwitchJoinOrderHifi`, `WallJoinsHifi`, `PaintMaterialHifi`, `MeasureBetweenHifi`, `MeasureAlongHifi`, `UngroupHifi`

input.description: hifi-collaboration.tsx: 10 collaboration icons
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-collaboration.tsx` with 10 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op    paper/plaster (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      alert (terracotta)
```
2+ fill zones per icon. Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons

### ReloadLatestHifi
Reload / sync latest model from central. A circular refresh arrow (two arcs forming a circle) with an arrowhead. Inside the circle: a small simplified model/building (rectangular plan, `--hi-concrete` walls). The arrow ring: `--hi-steel` fill (the arc body shown as a thin filled band). A small download arrow at bottom.

### EditingRequestHifi
Request to edit a workset element. A wall element (`--hi-concrete`) with a "lock" icon that is being unlocked — padlock with open shackle. An "Edit" request badge (small rectangle, `--hi-steel` fill) with a cursor/hand pointer. The wall shows it's currently owned by another user (different fill color).

### CopyMonitorHifi
Copy/Monitor linked element. Two model boxes side by side (like LinkedModelHifi). One box (`--hi-concrete`) is the host model, the other (`--hi-steel`) is the linked model. A monitoring arrow from the linked element to the local copy, with a small eye symbol. A sync/link line connecting the two.

### CoordinationReviewHifi
Coordination review process. A 3D model view with two element categories overlaid (structural `--hi-concrete`, MEP `--hi-steel`). A clash/conflict zone highlighted in `--hi-roof`. A checkmark or review status indicator (clipboard with check mark, `--hi-finish`).

### PurgeUnusedHifi
Purge unused families/elements. A waste bin or recycle arrow. Inside the bin: several small unused element symbols (generic shapes) in gray/faded fills. The bin: `--hi-steel` body. One item being swept into the bin. Cleans the model by removing unused content.

### TransferProjectStandardsHifi
Transfer standards between projects. Two document boxes (project A and project B). An arrow flowing from one to the other carrying "standards" (small consistent symbols like a dimension annotation, a text style). Project A: `--hi-concrete` fill, Project B: `--hi-finish`. The transfer arrow: bold.

### ProjectInfoHifi
Project information. A formal document (`--hi-finish` paper, `--hi-steel` header). Inside: a building thumbnail on the left, project metadata lines on the right (multiple horizontal placeholder lines). A project number badge at top. Bold professional border.

### ProjectParametersHifi
Project parameters/properties. A configuration panel: `--hi-finish` background with `--hi-steel` header. Inside: 3 parameter rows each showing a label stub (short line) and a value zone (`--hi-room` fill). A small gear/settings icon in the header.

### SharedParametersHifi
Shared parameters (common definitions across projects). A central parameter definition (document, `--hi-finish`) with arrows pointing to 3 different project boxes (`--hi-concrete`, `--hi-steel`, `--hi-room`). The shared nature shown by the radiating connections from one source.

### ObjectStylesHifi
Object styles/categories. A mini palette or style panel showing 3–4 layer/category bars, each with a different line weight and fill: bold line (`--hi-concrete`), medium line (`--hi-steel`), thin line (`--hi-finish`), and a pattern fill zone (`--hi-room`). A color swatch at each row end.

## Output
Required exports: `ReloadLatestHifi`, `EditingRequestHifi`, `CopyMonitorHifi`, `CoordinationReviewHifi`, `PurgeUnusedHifi`, `TransferProjectStandardsHifi`, `ProjectInfoHifi`, `ProjectParametersHifi`, `SharedParametersHifi`, `ObjectStylesHifi`

Shared parameters (common definitions across projects). A central parameter definition (document, `--hi-finish`) with arrows pointing to 3 different project boxes (`--hi-concrete`, `--hi-steel`, `--hi-room`). The shared nature shown by the radiating connections from one source.

Object styles/categories. A mini palette or style panel showing 3–4 layer/category bars, each with a different line weight and fill: bold line (`--hi-concrete`), medium line (`--hi-steel`), thin line (`--hi-finish`), and a pattern fill zone (`--hi-room`). A color swatch at each row end.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op    plaster/paper (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber/wood (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      roof/alert (terracotta)
```
2+ fill zones per icon. Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons

### WallSweepHifi
A wall sweep (molding/profile along a wall). Show a wall cross-section (`--hi-concrete`) with a decorative profile attached to one face. The sweep profile: a small L/S-shaped extrusion on the wall face in `--hi-finish` or `--hi-leaf` (wood molding). Bold wall outline, and the profile shown in detail on the face.

### WallRevealHifi
A wall reveal (recessed groove in wall face). Show a wall cross-section (`--hi-concrete`, hatched). On the face: a visible indented groove — a narrow rectangular recess in `--hi-room` (shadow/void). The reveal profile clearly visible. The outer wall face smooth.

### ComponentHifi
Generic BIM component/family instance. Draw a simplified parametric element — e.g., a furniture piece (desk chair plan): `--hi-leaf` for wood seat, `--hi-steel` for metal legs (4 small circles in corners). A bounding box around it (dashed rectangle). Small grip handles at corners (solid squares, `--hi-steel`).

### RoofExtrusionHifi
Extruded roof (roof by extrusion). Show a roof cross-section profile — a curved or pitched shape being extruded. The profile: `--hi-roof` fill. An extrusion arrow pointing out of the profile plane. The extruded volume hint in lighter `--hi-roof` opacity.

### RoofSoffitHifi
Roof soffit (the underside of a roof overhang). Show an overhang cross-section: the roof above (`--hi-roof`), the soffit board below (`--hi-leaf` for wood soffit or `--hi-finish`). The eave space between them. A fascia board at the edge. Looking up at the underside.

### FasciaHifi
Fascia board (the board that runs along the eave). An eave detail cross-section: roof rafters/deck above (`--hi-roof`), fascia board at the front edge (`--hi-leaf`), gutter below (`--hi-steel`). The fascia is the prominent element — a flat vertical board connecting roof to gutter.

### GutterHifi
Roof gutter system. A half-round or box gutter cross-section (`--hi-steel`). The gutter hangs below the fascia (`--hi-leaf` at top). Rain/water represented by `--hi-glass` fill inside the gutter bowl. A downspout connection at one end. Clean cross-section view.

### CurtainSystemHifi
Curtain wall system (the overall system, not just one panel). Plan view: a curved or angled curtain wall run with multiple panels. Glass panes (`--hi-glass`), mullions (`--hi-steel`), and the building interior beyond (`--hi-room`). The system as a whole — multiple bays visible.

### CurtainGridHifi
Curtain wall grid (mullion grid pattern). Elevation/plan view of the grid only: a matrix of horizontal and vertical lines forming a regular panel grid. The grid nodes (intersections): small `--hi-steel` filled circles. The panels defined by the grid: `--hi-glass` fill. Bold grid lines (1.5) for mullions.

## Output
Required exports: `WallSweepHifi`, `WallRevealHifi`, `ComponentHifi`, `RoofExtrusionHifi`, `RoofSoffitHifi`, `FasciaHifi`, `GutterHifi`, `CurtainSystemHifi`, `CurtainGridHifi`

input.description: hifi-arch-ext-b.tsx: 10 architectural extended icons (second half)
input.prompt:
Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/src/hifi-arch-ext-b.tsx` with 10 high-fidelity 48×48 BIM icons. New file — use Write tool. DO NOT touch index.ts.

## Setup
```tsx
import { bimIconHifi } from './icon';
```
48×48 viewBox. SVG root: `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round"`. ~4px margin.

## CSS variables
```
--hi-concrete/op  concrete (stone gray)
--hi-finish/op    plaster/paper (warm white)
--hi-glass/op     glazing (sky blue)
--hi-steel/op     steel/metal (blue-gray)
--hi-leaf         timber/wood (amber, no op var)
--hi-room/op      interior zone (pale yellow)
--hi-roof/op      roof/alert (terracotta)
```
2+ fill zones per icon. Stroke: 2=primary, 1.5=secondary, 0.75=detail.

## Icons

### VerticalOpeningHifi
A vertical opening through a floor slab. Section view: floor slab (`--hi-concrete`, hatched) with a rectangular void cut through it. The void: `--hi-room` fill with diagonal hatch or X pattern indicating void. Bold slab boundary. Dashed lines at void edges. Small drop arrows.

### DormerOpeningHifi
A dormer window in a roof (plan or section view). Roof slope (`--hi-roof`) with a perpendicular bump containing a window. The dormer walls: `--hi-finish`. The dormer window: `--hi-glass`. Section shows the dormer protruding from the main roof slope.

### FaceOpeningHifi
An opening cut in a wall face (face-based). Plan view: wall section (`--hi-concrete`, hatched) with an irregular opening shape cut through it. The opening: `--hi-room` fill. The opening boundary is shown with bold lines and dashed extent markers.

### RoomSeparatorHifi
A room separator line (invisible boundary dividing rooms). Two adjacent room areas: left room (`--hi-room` fill), right room (slightly different `--hi-finish` fill). Between them: a thin dashed line (the separator) with small triangle markers. No wall — just the invisible separation.

### AreaBoundaryHifi
Area boundary (for area plans). A closed boundary line separating different area zones. Interior of area A: `--hi-room`. Interior of area B: `--hi-finish`. The boundary line: bold dashed, with small boundary markers at corners. An area label badge inside each zone.

### ReferencePlaneHifi
A reference plane (datum plane). A large flat plane shown in 3D isometric: a rectangle with `--hi-steel` at very low opacity (transparent plane). Bold edges (strokeWidth 1.5). A name label on the plane. A small perpendicular indicator arrow showing the plane's normal direction.

### WorkPlaneHifi
Work plane (active sketching/drawing plane). Similar to ReferencePlane but with a grid overlay. The plane: `--hi-glass` at very low opacity. A grid of thin lines (0.75) on the plane surface. A bold normal arrow. A small sketch cursor/pencil symbol on the plane.

### ModelLineHifi
A model line (3D line in the model). A bold 3D line in perspective or isometric space, running diagonally. The line body shown as a thin filled rectangle (`--hi-steel` fill). Small endpoint spheres at each end. The 3D context implied by dashed projection lines.

### ModelTextHifi
Model text (3D text in the model). A 3D isometric text "ABC" placeholder sitting on a work plane. The text shown as simplified bold letterforms. The work plane below: `--hi-finish` at low opacity. The text itself: `--hi-steel` fill for the letter bodies.

### StairPathHifi
Stair path (the walking path annotation on a stair). Plan view of a stair outline (`--hi-concrete`) with the walking path overlaid: a curved or diagonal dashed path with small directional arrows and numbered step markers (1, 2, 3, ...). The path is the prominent feature.

## Output
Required exports: `VerticalOpeningHifi`, `DormerOpeningHifi`, `FaceOpeningHifi`, `RoomSeparatorHifi`, `AreaBoundaryHifi`, `ReferencePlaneHifi`, `WorkPlaneHifi`, `ModelLineHifi`, `ModelTextHifi`, `StairPathHifi`

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User wants ALL ~218 regular 24×24 BIM icons to have hi-fi 48×48 counterparts (not just a subset)
   - Each icon should exist in three color variants: Mono, Accent, Color
   - The gallery page at `/icons` should group by icon identity (Wall shows regular + all hifi variants together), NOT by resolution type (separate "regular" and "hifi" sections)
   - Heavily parallelize all work using background agents
   - Previously: redesign existing 10 hifi icons to have multi-material fill zones so Color mode shows genuinely distinct materials

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - `bimIconHifi` factory: creates 48×48 SVG components from `./icon.tsx`; `viewBox="0 0 48 48"`, no global strokeWidth scaling
   - CSS custom property fill system: `--hi-concrete/op`, `--hi-finish/op`, `--hi-glass/op`, `--hi-steel/op`, `--hi-leaf` (no op), `--hi-roof/op`, `--hi-room/op`
   - Multi-material fill zones: each icon MUST have 2+ distinct fill zones using different CSS variables
   - Stroke hierarchy: 2=primary outlines, 1.5=secondary edges, 0.75=hatch/detail
   - Three gallery color schemes: Mono (no vars), Accent (all amber `#d97706`), Color (material-accurate palette)
   - `HIFI_SECTIONS` categorized structure (replacing flat `HIFI_ICONS` array) with `ALL_HIFI_ICONS` flat alias
   - Pattern: `style={{ fill: 'var(--hi-concrete, currentColor)', fillOpacity: 'var(--hi-concrete-op, 0.07)' }}`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `packages/icons/src/icon.tsx`: `bimIconHifi` factory producing 48×48 SVG components; `BimIconHifiProps` type (SVGAttributes + size only)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi.tsx`: 10 redesigned base icons with multi-material zones (Wall=3-layer, Door=concrete+wood, Window=concrete+wood+glass, Stair=concrete+finish strips, Column=concrete+steel rebar, Beam=concrete slab+steel I-beam, Roof=tiles+wood fascia, Room=concrete walls+interior, Section=concrete cut+marker, Floor=screed topping+slab)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-architectural.tsx`: 9 icons (Railing, CurtainWall, Ceiling, Opening, Ramp, Partition, Tag, Dimension, Mass)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-mep.tsx`: 8 icons (DuctRect, DuctRound, Pipe, CableTray, MechanicalEquipment, PlumbingFixture, LightingFixture, MepSpace)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-structural.tsx`: 6 icons (Foundation, StripFooting, Truss, Brace, Rebar, StructuralConnection)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-views.tsx`: 8 icons (PlanView, SectionView, ElevationView, Sheet, ScheduleView, Callout, GridLine, Level)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-organization.tsx`: 8 icons (Family, FamilyType, Group, LinkedModel, Material, Phase, Issue, Clash)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-site-annotation.tsx`: 8 icons (Topo, PropertyLine, NorthArrow, RevisionCloud, ScaleBar, AreaLabel, Keynote, SpotElevation)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-misc-a.tsx` (DONE): 8 icons (OrbitView, Viewpoint, SectionBox, DetailLine, Assembly, WallLayer, ValidationRule, Deviation)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-misc-b.tsx` (DONE): 8 icons (Conduit, ElectricalPanel, FireSprinkler, Diffuser, ShaftOpening, CurtainPanel, Mullion, SkyLight)
   - `packages/icons/src/hifi-misc-c.tsx` (DONE): 8 icons (ParkingSpace, Planting, Road, RetainingWall, BreakLine, CentreLine, MatchLine, SlopeArrow)
   - `packages/icons/src/index.ts`: exports from `./hifi`, `./hifi-architectural`, `./hifi-mep`, `./hifi-structural`, `./hifi-views`, `./hifi-organization`, `./hifi-site-annotation` (plus all regular icon files)
   - `packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx`: restructured with `HIFI_SECTIONS` (6 categories), `ALL_HIFI_ICONS` flat alias, header shows `{totalCount} 24px icons · {ALL_HIFI_ICONS.length} hi-fi icons`, color scheme vars include `--hi-finish` and `--hi-finish-op`

   Color scheme values in IconGallery.tsx:
   ```js
   // Accent:
   '--hi-finish': '#d97706', '--hi-finish-op': '0.09'
   // Color:
   '--hi-finish': '#f0ead8', '--hi-finish-op': '0.85'
   '--hi-concrete': '#c8c4be', '--hi-concrete-op': '0.45'
   '--hi-glass': '#7dd3fc', '--hi-glass-op': '0.5'
   '--hi-steel': '#94a3b8', '--hi-steel-op': '0.4'
   '--hi-leaf': '#a16207'
   '--hi-roof': '#f87171', '--hi-roof-op': '0.38'
   '--hi-room': '#fef3c7', '--hi-room-op': '0.5'
   ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - Color mode looked "one color only": Root cause was 7/10 icons having only ONE fill material zone. Fixed by redesigning all icons to have 2+ distinct fill zones using different CSS variables.
   - MEP agent and gallery-wiring agent both attempted to update index.ts in the first dispatch: resulted in correct non-duplicate state (grepped index.ts showed exactly one block per hifi file). All subsequent agent prompts explicitly say "DO NOT touch index.ts".
   - Pre-existing TypeScript errors in web package (offlineQueue.test.ts, ImageTraceDropZone.tsx, PlanCanvas.tsx, ProjectBrowser.test.tsx) — unrelated to icon work, not fixed.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Multi-material issue: Solved by redesigning icons to have distinct material zones (finish face + concrete core + finish face for walls; concrete slab + steel I-section for beams; etc.)
   - Heavy parallelization: Each agent writes to a UNIQUE new file, preventing file conflicts. index.ts and IconGallery.tsx updates are handled by dedicated wiring agents.
   - Gallery layout: User wants icon identity grouping (Wall regular + Wall hifi together). Plan is to redesign IconGallery.tsx so each icon card shows both the regular 24px icon and hifi variants. Gallery redesign agent is still pending dispatch.

6. All user messages:
   - "yep" (approving the plan to redesign icons with multiple material zones)
   - "can you generally structure the icons into regular and hifi, and then hifi into mono, accent and colored and do the work for the rest of the icons? please parallelize HEAVILY, because this can perfectly be parallelized in dispatching"
   - "why only 49 hifi, not 248? i want for each one all variant; also think of a better layout in the page itself, grouping them better by the actual icon, not hifi/regular etc"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Create remaining hifi icon files (still running or not yet dispatched):
     - `hifi-workflow.tsx` (14 icons: Workset, DesignOption, Revision, RFI, Submittal, PointCloud, DigitalTwin, Sync, Transmittal, IFC, LOD, QuantityTakeoff, EnergyModel, ScopeBox) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-edit-ops.tsx` (9 icons: Mirror, ArrayLinear, ArrayRadial, Align, Split, TrimExtend, Void, Pin, Unpin) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-edit-basic.tsx` (14 icons: Move, Copy, Rotate, Scale, Offset, Delete, JoinGeometry, UnjoinGeometry, SwitchJoinOrder, WallJoins, PaintMaterial, MeasureBetween, MeasureAlong, Ungroup) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-collaboration.tsx` (10 icons: ReloadLatest through ObjectStyles) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-arch-ext-a.tsx` (9 icons: WallSweep through CurtainGrid) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-arch-ext-b.tsx` (10 icons: VerticalOpening through StairPath) — RUNNING
     - `hifi-structural-ext.tsx` (13 icons: BeamSystem through BoundaryCondition) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
     - `hifi-mep-ext-a.tsx` (12 icons: FlexDuct through ElectricalWire) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
     - `hifi-mep-ext-b.tsx` (12 icons: SwitchSystem through Valve) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
     - `hifi-views-ext.tsx` (13 icons: ReflectedCeilingPlan through ViewReference) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
     - `hifi-annotation-ext-a.tsx` (11 icons: AngularDimension through InsulationAnnotation) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
     - `hifi-annotation-ext-b.tsx` (10 icons: TextAnnotation through ReferencePoint) — NOT YET DISPATCHED
   - Update `packages/icons/src/index.ts` to export all new hifi files (NOT YET DISPATCHED)
   - Redesign `IconGallery.tsx` to group by icon identity: each icon card shows regular 24px + hifi variants (mono/accent/color) together — NOT YET DISPATCHED

8. Current Work:
   Mid-execution of second parallel dispatch wave. 9 agents were sent simultaneously:
   - 3 completed: `hifi-misc-a.tsx`, `hifi-misc-b.tsx`, `hifi-misc-c.tsx`
   - 6 still running: `hifi-workflow.tsx`, `hifi-edit-ops.tsx`, `hifi-edit-basic.tsx`, `hifi-collaboration.tsx`, `hifi-arch-ext-a.tsx`, `hifi-arch-ext-b.tsx`

   The summary was triggered mid-flight (context compression triggered during the parallel dispatch).

9. Optional Next Step:
   Wait for the 6 currently running agents to complete, then dispatch the remaining 9 agents (6 icon files + index.ts wiring + gallery layout redesign + final gallery mapping). The gallery redesign should implement: "a better layout in the page itself, grouping them better by the actual icon, not hifi/regular etc" — meaning each icon card shows its regular 24px icon alongside all three hifi color variants, removing the separate hifi top section.

   After all icon files exist, a final wiring agent must:
   1. Update `packages/icons/src/index.ts` with exports from all 15 new hifi files
   2. Update `packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx` so the `SECTIONS` data includes optional `Hifi` references for all 218 icons, and the card component renders both regular + hifi variants

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/jhoetter/.claude/projects/-Users-jhoetter-repos-bim-ai/965188de-05c1-45fb-bdf6-79ca31696284.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

file.content:
/**
 * @bim-ai/icons — BIM-native SVG icon library.
 *
 * Drop-in compatible with lucide-react's icon API:
 *
 *   import { WallIcon, DoorIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons'
 *   <WallIcon size={18} strokeWidth={1.5} className="text-stone-600" />
 *
 * All icons are 24×24 viewBox, stroke-based, currentColor.
 * Props: size, strokeWidth, absoluteStrokeWidth, plus any SVG attribute.
 */

export type { BimIcon, BimIconProps, BimIconHifiProps } from './icon';

// High-fidelity 48×48 icons
export {
  WallHifi,
  DoorHifi,
  WindowHifi,
  StairHifi,
  ColumnHifi,
  BeamHifi,
  RoofHifi,
  RoomHifi,
  SectionHifi,
  FloorHifi,
} from './hifi';

// Architectural hifi
export {
  RailingHifi,
  CurtainWallHifi,
  CeilingHifi,
  OpeningHifi,
  RampHifi,
  PartitionHifi,
  TagHifi,
  DimensionHifi,
  MassHifi,
} from './hifi-architectural';

// MEP hifi
export {
  DuctRectHifi,
  DuctRoundHifi,
  PipeHifi,
  CableTrayHifi,
  MechanicalEquipmentHifi,
  PlumbingFixtureHifi,
  LightingFixtureHifi,
  MepSpaceHifi,
} from './hifi-mep';

// Structural hifi
export {
  FoundationHifi,
  StripFootingHifi,
  TrussHifi,
  BraceHifi,
  RebarHifi,
  StructuralConnectionHifi,
} from './hifi-structural';

// Views hifi
export {
  PlanViewHifi,
  SectionViewHifi,
  ElevationViewHifi,
  SheetHifi,
  ScheduleViewHifi,
  CalloutHifi,
  GridLineHifi,
  LevelHifi,
} from './hifi-views';

// Organization hifi
export {
  FamilyHifi,
  FamilyTypeHifi,
  GroupHifi,
  LinkedModelHifi,
  MaterialHifi,
  PhaseHifi,
  IssueHifi,
  ClashHifi,
} from './hifi-organization';

// Site & annotation hifi
export {
  TopoHifi,
  PropertyLineHifi,
  NorthArrowHifi,
  RevisionCloudHifi,
  ScaleBarHifi,
  AreaLabelHifi,
  KeynoteHifi,
  SpotElevationHifi,
} from './hifi-site-annotation';

// Phase 1 — drawing tools
export {
  WallIcon,
  DoorIcon,
  WindowIcon,
  FloorIcon,
  RoofIcon,
  StairIcon,
  RailingIcon,
  RoomIcon,
  DimensionIcon,
  SectionIcon,
  TagIcon,
  CurtainWallIcon,
  ColumnIcon,
  BeamIcon,
} from './tools';

// Phase 2 — views & annotations (original)
export {
  PlanViewIcon,
  SectionViewIcon,
  ElevationViewIcon,
  OrbitViewIcon,
  SheetIcon,
  ScheduleViewIcon,
  CalloutIcon,
  ViewpointIcon,
  SectionBoxIcon,
  GridLineIcon,
  LevelIcon,
  DetailLineIcon,
} from './views';

// Phase 3 — organization & coordination (original)
export {
  FamilyIcon,
  FamilyTypeIcon,
  GroupIcon,
  AssemblyIcon,
  LinkedModelIcon,
  MaterialIcon,
  WallLayerIcon,
  PhaseIcon,
  IssueIcon,
  ClashIcon,
  ValidationRuleIcon,
  DeviationIcon,
} from './organization';

// MEP — mechanical, electrical & plumbing
export {
  DuctRectIcon,
  DuctRoundIcon,
  PipeIcon,
  CableTrayIcon,
  ConduitIcon,
  MechanicalEquipmentIcon,
  PlumbingFixtureIcon,
  LightingFixtureIcon,
  ElectricalPanelIcon,
  FireSprinklerIcon,
  DiffuserIcon,
  MepSpaceIcon,
} from './mep';

// Structural
export {
  FoundationIcon,
  StripFootingIcon,
  TrussIcon,
  BraceIcon,
  RebarIcon,
  StructuralConnectionIcon,
} from './structural';

// Architectural additions
export {
  CeilingIcon,
  OpeningIcon,
  ShaftOpeningIcon,
  RampIcon,
  MassIcon,
  CurtainPanelIcon,
  MullionIcon,
  SkyLightIcon,
  PartitionIcon,
} from './architectural';

// Site & civil
export {
  TopoIcon,
  PropertyLineIcon,
  ParkingSpaceIcon,
  PlantingIcon,
  RoadIcon,
  RetainingWallIcon,
  NorthArrowIcon,
} from './site';

// Annotation & documentation
export {
  RevisionCloudIcon,
  BreakLineIcon,
  CentreLineIcon,
  KeynoteIcon,
  MatchLineIcon,
  ScaleBarIcon,
  AreaLabelIcon,
  SpotElevationIcon,
  SlopeArrowIcon,
} from './annotation';

// Workflow, coordination & data
export {
  WorksetIcon,
  DesignOptionIcon,
  RevisionIcon,
  RFIIcon,
  SubmittalIcon,
  PointCloudIcon,
  DigitalTwinIcon,
  SyncIcon,
  TransmittalIcon,
  IFCIcon,
  LODIcon,
  QuantityTakeoffIcon,
  EnergyModelIcon,
  ScopeBoxIcon,
} from './workflow';

// Edit / geometry operations
export {
  MirrorIcon,
  ArrayLinearIcon,
  ArrayRadialIcon,
  AlignIcon,
  SplitIcon,
  TrimExtendIcon,
  VoidIcon,
  PinIcon,
  UnpinIcon,
} from './edit';

// Architectural extended
export {
  WallSweepIcon,
  WallRevealIcon,
  ComponentIcon,
  RoofExtrusionIcon,
  RoofSoffitIcon,
  FasciaIcon,
  GutterIcon,
  CurtainSystemIcon,
  CurtainGridIcon,
  VerticalOpeningIcon,
  DormerOpeningIcon,
  FaceOpeningIcon,
  RoomSeparatorIcon,
  AreaBoundaryIcon,
  ReferencePlaneIcon,
  WorkPlaneIcon,
  ModelLineIcon,
  ModelTextIcon,
  StairPathIcon,
} from './architectural-extended';

// Structural extended
export {
  BeamSystemIcon,
  FoundationSlabIcon,
  SlabEdgeIcon,
  AreaReinforcementIcon,
  PathReinforcementIcon,
  FabricSheetIcon,
  ShearStudIcon,
  AnchorIcon,
  BoltIcon,
  WeldIcon,
  AnalyticalNodeIcon,
  StructuralLoadIcon,
  BoundaryConditionIcon,
} from './structural-extended';

// MEP extended
export {
  FlexDuctIcon,
  DuctFittingIcon,
  DuctAccessoryIcon,
  DuctInsulationIcon,
  DuctLiningIcon,
  FlexPipeIcon,
  PipeFittingIcon,
  PipeAccessoryIcon,
  PipeInsulationIcon,
  CableTrayFittingIcon,
  ConduitFittingIcon,
  ElectricalWireIcon,
  SwitchSystemIcon,
  LightingSwitchIcon,
  CommunicationDeviceIcon,
  FireAlarmDeviceIcon,
  DataDeviceIcon,
  NurseCallIcon,
  SecurityDeviceIcon,
  HVACZoneIcon,
  PanelScheduleIcon,
  DuctSystemIcon,
  PipingSystemIcon,
  ValveIcon,
} from './mep-extended';

// Views extended
export {
  ReflectedCeilingPlanIcon,
  StructuralPlanIcon,
  AreaPlanIcon,
  DetailViewIcon,
  DraftingViewIcon,
  WalkthroughIcon,
  LegendIcon,
  SheetListIcon,
  NoteBlockIcon,
  ViewListIcon,
  PanelScheduleViewIcon,
  GraphicalColumnScheduleIcon,
  ViewReferenceIcon,
} from './views-extended';

// Annotation extended
export {
  AngularDimensionIcon,
  RadialDimensionIcon,
  ArcLengthDimensionIcon,
  OrdinalDimensionIcon,
  SpotCoordinateIcon,
  ElevationTagIcon,
  FilledRegionIcon,
  MaskingRegionIcon,
  DetailComponentIcon,
  RepeatingDetailIcon,
  InsulationAnnotationIcon,
  TextAnnotationIcon,
  MultiCategoryTagIcon,
  MaterialTagIcon,
  RoomTagIcon,
  SpaceTagIcon,
  AreaTagIcon,
  StairTagIcon,
  SymbolIcon,
  StairPathAnnotationIcon,
  ReferencePointIcon,
} from './annotation-extended';

// Edit basic / modify operations
export {
  MoveIcon,
  CopyIcon,
  RotateIcon,
  ScaleIcon,
  OffsetIcon,
  DeleteIcon,
  JoinGeometryIcon,
  UnjoinGeometryIcon,
  SwitchJoinOrderIcon,
  WallJoinsIcon,
  PaintMaterialIcon,
  MeasureBetweenIcon,
  MeasureAlongIcon,
  UngroupIcon,
} from './edit-basic';

// Collaboration & project management
export {
  ReloadLatestIcon,
  EditingRequestIcon,
  CopyMonitorIcon,
  CoordinationReviewIcon,
  PurgeUnusedIcon,
  TransferProjectStandardsIcon,
  ProjectInfoIcon,
  ProjectParametersIcon,
  SharedParametersIcon,
  ObjectStylesIcon,
} from './collaboration';

681	
682	const ALL_ICONS = SECTIONS.flatMap((s) => s.icons);
683	
684	function HiFiCard({ entry }: { entry: HiFiEntry }) {
685	  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
686	
687	  const handleClick = useCallback(() => {
688	    const text = `import { ${entry.export} } from '@bim-ai/icons'`;
689	    navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
690	      setCopied(true);
691	      setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1400);
692	    });
693	  }, [entry.export]);
694	
695	  return (
696	    <button
697	      type="button"
698	      onClick={handleClick}
699	      title={`Click to copy import for ${entry.export}`}
700	      className="group relative flex flex-col items-center gap-3 rounded-xl border border-border bg-surface p-5 text-center transition-all hover:border-accent hover:bg-accent-soft focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent"
701	    >
702	      {copied && (
703	        <span className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-accent text-xs font-medium text-accent-foreground">
704	          Copied!
705	        </span>
706	      )}
707	      <div className="flex h-14 w-14 items-center justify-center">
708	        <entry.Icon
709	          size={48}
710	          className="text-foreground transition-colors group-hover:text-accent"
711	        />
712	      </div>
713	      <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">{entry.name}</span>
714	      <span className="absolute -top-7 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 whitespace-nowrap rounded bg-foreground px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-[10px] text-background opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100">
715	        {entry.export}
716	      </span>
717	    </button>
718	  );
719	}
720	
721	const SIZES = [16, 18, 20, 24, 32] as const;
722	type Size = (typeof SIZES)[number];
723	
724	const STROKE_WIDTHS = [1, 1.5, 2] as const;
725	type StrokeWidth = (typeof STROKE_WIDTHS)[number];
726	
727	function IconCard({
728	  entry,
729	  size,
730	  strokeWidth,
731	}: {
732	  entry: IconEntry;
733	  size: Size;
734	  strokeWidth: StrokeWidth;
735	}) {
736	  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
737	
738	  const handleClick = useCallback(() => {
739	    const text = `import { ${entry.export} } from '@bim-ai/icons'`;
740	    navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
741	      setCopied(true);
742	      setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1400);
743	    });
744	  }, [entry.export]);
745	
746	  return (
747	    <button
748	      type="button"
749	      onClick={handleClick}
750	      title={`Click to copy import for ${entry.export}`}
751	      className="group relative flex flex-col items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-border bg-surface p-4 text-center transition-all hover:border-accent hover:bg-accent-soft focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent"
752	    >
753	      {copied && (
754	        <span className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-accent text-xs font-medium text-accent-foreground">
755	          Copied!
756	        </span>
757	      )}
758	      <entry.Icon
759	        size={size}
760	        strokeWidth={strokeWidth}
761	        className="text-foreground transition-colors group-hover:text-accent"
762	      />
763	      <span className="max-w-full truncate text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">{entry.name}</span>
764	      <span className="absolute -top-7 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 whitespace-nowrap rounded bg-foreground px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-[10px] text-background opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100">
765	        {entry.export}
766	      </span>
767	    </button>
768	  );
769	}
770	
771	export function IconGallery() {
772	  const [query, setQuery] = useState('');
773	  const [size, setSize] = useState<Size>(24);
774	  const [strokeWidth, setStrokeWidth] = useState<StrokeWidth>(1.5);
775	
776	  const filtered = useMemo(() => {
777	    const q = query.toLowerCase().trim();
778	    if (!q) return null;
779	    return ALL_ICONS.filter(
780	      (e) => e.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) || e.export.toLowerCase().includes(q),
781	    );
782	  }, [query]);
783	
784	  const totalCount = ALL_ICONS.length;
785	
786	  return (
787	    <div className="min-h-screen bg-background text-foreground">
788	      {/* sticky header */}
789	      <div className="sticky top-0 z-10 border-b border-border bg-background/90 backdrop-blur">
790	        <div className="mx-auto max-w-5xl px-6 py-4">
791	          <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-4">
792	            <div>
793	              <h1 className="text-base font-semibold">@bim-ai/icons</h1>
794	              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
795	                {totalCount} 24px icons · {ALL_HIFI_ICONS.length} hi-fi icons
796	              </p>
797	            </div>
798	
799	            <input
800	              type="search"
801	              value={query}
802	              onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
803	              placeholder="Search icons…"
804	              className="h-8 w-56 rounded-md border border-border bg-surface px-3 text-sm placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent"
805	            />
806	
807	            <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
808	              <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
809	                <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">size</span>
810	                <div className="flex rounded-md border border-border">
811	                  {SIZES.map((s, i) => (
812	                    <button
813	                      type="button"
814	                      key={s}
815	                      onClick={() => setSize(s)}
816	                      className={[
817	                        'px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-mono transition-colors',
818	                        s === size
819	                          ? 'bg-foreground text-background'
820	                          : 'text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground',
821	                        i === 0 ? 'rounded-l-sm' : '',
822	                        i === SIZES.length - 1 ? 'rounded-r-sm' : '',
823	                      ]
824	                        .filter(Boolean)
825	                        .join(' ')}
826	                    >
827	                      {s}
828	                    </button>
829	                  ))}
830	                </div>
831	              </div>
832	
833	              <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
834	                <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">stroke</span>
835	                <div className="flex rounded-md border border-border">
836	                  {STROKE_WIDTHS.map((sw, i) => (
837	                    <button
838	                      type="button"
839	                      key={sw}
840	                      onClick={() => setStrokeWidth(sw)}
841	                      className={[
842	                        'px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-mono transition-colors',
843	                        sw === strokeWidth
844	                          ? 'bg-foreground text-background'
845	                          : 'text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground',
846	                        i === 0 ? 'rounded-l-sm' : '',
847	                        i === STROKE_WIDTHS.length - 1 ? 'rounded-r-sm' : '',
848	                      ]
849	                        .filter(Boolean)
850	                        .join(' ')}
851	                    >
852	                      {sw}
853	                    </button>
854	                  ))}
855	                </div>
856	              </div>
857	            </div>
858	          </div>
859	        </div>
860	      </div>
861	
862	      <div className="mx-auto max-w-5xl px-6 py-8">
863	        {/* High-fidelity section — always visible */}
864	        <section className="mb-12">
865	          <div className="mb-1 flex items-center gap-3">
866	            <h2 className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest text-muted-foreground">
867	              High-fidelity 48×48
868	            </h2>
869	            <span className="rounded-full bg-accent-soft px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-medium text-accent">
870	              new
871	            </span>
872	          </div>
873	          <p className="mb-6 text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
874	            Fills are driven by CSS custom properties — set variables on a wrapper to switch
875	            variants. Hatch lines always use <code className="font-mono">currentColor</code>.
876	          </p>
877	
878	          <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-6">
879	            {(
880	              [
881	                {
882	                  label: 'Mono',
883	                  description: 'Adapts to any theme',
884	                  vars: {},
885	                },
886	                {
887	                  label: 'Accent',
888	                  description: 'Single accent colour',
889	                  vars: {
890	                    '--hi-concrete': '#d97706',
891	                    '--hi-concrete-op': '0.14',
892	                    '--hi-glass': '#d97706',
893	                    '--hi-glass-op': '0.22',
894	                    '--hi-steel': '#d97706',
895	                    '--hi-steel-op': '0.18',
896	                    '--hi-finish': '#d97706',
897	                    '--hi-finish-op': '0.09',
898	                    '--hi-leaf': '#b45309',
899	                    '--hi-roof': '#d97706',
900	                    '--hi-roof-op': '0.13',
901	                    '--hi-room': '#d97706',
902	                    '--hi-room-op': '0.07',
903	                  },
904	                },
905	                {
906	                  label: 'Color',
907	                  description: 'Material-accurate palette',
908	                  vars: {
909	                    // concrete: warm stone gray, semi-transparent so hatch reads through
910	                    '--hi-concrete': '#c8c4be',
911	                    '--hi-concrete-op': '0.45',
912	                    // glass: sky blue, airy and transparent-feeling
913	                    '--hi-glass': '#7dd3fc',
914	                    '--hi-glass-op': '0.5',
915	                    // steel: blue-gray, lighter than concrete
916	                    '--hi-steel': '#94a3b8',
917	                    '--hi-steel-op': '0.4',
918	                    // plaster/screed finish face: warm off-white
919	                    '--hi-finish': '#f0ead8',
920	                    '--hi-finish-op': '0.85',
921	                    // door leaf: warm amber-brown wood tone
922	                    '--hi-leaf': '#a16207',
923	                    // roof: terracotta red (not salmon pink)
924	                    '--hi-roof': '#f87171',
925	                    '--hi-roof-op': '0.38',
926	                    // room interior: pale warm yellow, plan-drawing convention
927	                    '--hi-room': '#fef3c7',
928	                    '--hi-room-op': '0.5',
929	                  },
930	                },
931	              ] as const
932	            ).map(({ label, description, vars }) => (
933	              <div key={label}>
934	                <div className="mb-3">
935	                  <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground">{label}</p>
936	                  <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">{description}</p>
937	                </div>
938	                <div className="space-y-5" style={vars as React.CSSProperties}>
939	                  {HIFI_SECTIONS.map((section) => (
940	                    <div key={section.label}>
941	                      <p className="mb-2 text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-muted-foreground/50">
942	                        {section.label}
943	                      </p>
944	                      <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
945	                        {section.icons.map((entry) => (
946	                          <HiFiCard key={entry.export} entry={entry} />
947	                        ))}
948	                      </div>
949	                    </div>
950	                  ))}
951	                </div>
952	              </div>
953	            ))}
954	          </div>
955	        </section>
956	
957	        <hr className="mb-10 border-border" />
958	
959	        {filtered !== null ? (
960	          <div>
961	            <p className="mb-4 text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
962	              {filtered.length === 0
963	                ? 'No icons match.'
964	                : `${filtered.length} result${filtered.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}`}
965	            </p>
966	            <div className="grid grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(100px,1fr))] gap-2">
967	              {filtered.map((entry) => (
968	                <IconCard key={entry.export} entry={entry} size={size} strokeWidth={strokeWidth} />
969	              ))}
970	            </div>
971	          </div>
972	        ) : (
973	          <div className="space-y-10">
974	            {SECTIONS.map((section) => (
975	              <section key={section.label}>
976	                <div className="mb-3 flex items-center gap-3">
977	                  <h2 className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest text-muted-foreground">
978	                    {section.label}
979	                  </h2>
980	                  <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground/60">
981	                    {section.icons.length}
982	                  </span>
983	                </div>
984	                <div className="grid grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(100px,1fr))] gap-2">
985	                  {section.icons.map((entry) => (
986	                    <IconCard
987	                      key={entry.export}
988	                      entry={entry}
989	                      size={size}
990	                      strokeWidth={strokeWidth}
991	                    />
992	                  ))}
993	                </div>
994	              </section>
995	            ))}
996	          </div>
997	        )}
998	
999	        <div className="mt-12 rounded-lg border border-border bg-surface p-5">
1000	          <p className="mb-2 text-xs font-semibold text-muted-foreground">Usage</p>
1001	          <pre className="overflow-x-auto rounded bg-surface-strong p-3 font-mono text-[12px] text-foreground">
1002	            {`import { WallIcon, DuctRectIcon, FoundationIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons'\n\n<WallIcon size={18} strokeWidth={1.5} className="text-stone-600" />`}
1003	          </pre>
1004	          <p className="mt-3 text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
1005	            Click any icon to copy its import statement. All icons accept{' '}
1006	            <code className="rounded bg-surface-strong px-1 font-mono">size</code>,{' '}
1007	            <code className="rounded bg-surface-strong px-1 font-mono">strokeWidth</code>, and any
1008	            SVG attribute.
1009	          </p>
1010	        </div>
1011	      </div>
1012	    </div>
1013	  );
1014	}
